data.description: Directly supervise and coordinate activities of workers engaged in firefighting and fire prevention and control

data.title: First-Line Supervisors of Security Workers
data.description: Directly supervise and coordinate activities of security workers and security guards

data.title: First-Line Supervisors of Protective Service Workers, All Other
data.description: All protective service supervisors not listed separately above

data.title: Firefighters
data.description: Control and extinguish fires or respond to emergency situations where life, property, or the environment is at risk. Duties may include fire prevention, emergency medical service, hazardous material response, search and rescue, and disaster assistance

data.title: Fire Inspectors and Investigators
data.description: Inspect buildings to detect fire hazards and enforce local ordinances and state laws, or investigate and gather facts to determine cause of fires and explosions

data.title: Forest Fire Inspectors and Prevention Specialists
data.description: Enforce fire regulations, inspect forest for fire hazards, and recommend forest fire prevention or control measures. May report forest fires and weather conditions

data.title: Bailiffs
data.description: Maintain order in courts of law

data.title: Correctional Officers and Jailers
data.description: Guard inmates in penal or rehabilitative institutions in accordance with established regulations and procedures. May guard prisoners in transit between jail, courtroom, prison, or other point. Includes deputy sheriffs and police who spend the majority of their time guarding prisoners in correctional institutions

data.title: Detectives and Criminal Investigators
data.description: Conduct investigations related to suspected violations of federal, state, or local laws to prevent or solve crimes

data.title: Police Identification and Records Officers
data.description: Collect evidence at crime scene, classify and identify fingerprints, and photograph evidence for use in criminal and civil cases

data.title: Intelligence Analysts
data.description: Gather, analyze, or evaluate information from a variety of sources, such as law enforcement databases, surveillance, intelligence networks or geographic information systems. Use intelligence data to anticipate and prevent organized crime activities, such as terrorism
alternate_titles: Crime and Intelligence Analyst (Criminal and Intelligence Analyst)
Police Crime and Intel Analyst (Police Crime and Intelligence Analyst)

data.title: Fish and Game Wardens
data.description: Patrol assigned area to prevent fish and game law violations. Investigate reports of damage to crops or property by wildlife. Compile biological data

data.title: Parking Enforcement Workers
data.description: Patrol assigned area, such as public parking lot or city streets to issue tickets to overtime parking violators and illegally parked vehicles

data.title: Police and Sheriff's Patrol Officers
data.description: Maintain order and protect life and property by enforcing local, tribal, state, or federal laws and ordinances. Perform a combination of the following duties: patrol a specific area; direct traffic; issue traffic summonses; investigate accidents; apprehend and arrest suspects, or serve legal processes of courts. Includes police officers working at educational institutions

data.title: Customs and Border Protection Officers
data.description: Investigate and inspect persons, common carriers, goods, and merchandise, arriving in or departing from the United States or between states to detect violations of immigration and customs laws and regulations
alternate_titles: US Customs and Border Protection Officer (US CBPO)

data.title: Transit and Railroad Police
data.description: Protect and police railroad and transit property, employees, or passengers

data.title: Animal Control Workers
data.description: Handle animals for the purpose of investigations of mistreatment, or control of abandoned, dangerous, or unattended animals

data.title: Private Detectives and Investigators
data.description: Gather, analyze, compile, and report information regarding individuals or organizations to clients, or detect occurrences of unlawful acts or infractions of rules in private establishment

data.title: Gambling Surveillance Officers and Gambling Investigators
data.description: Observe gambling operation for irregular activities such as cheating or theft by either employees or patrons. Investigate potential threats to gambling assets such as money, chips, and gambling equipment. Act as oversight and security agent for management and customers

data.title: Security Guards
data.description: Guard, patrol, or monitor premises to prevent theft, violence, or infractions of rules. May operate x-ray and metal detector equipment

data.title: Crossing Guards and Flaggers
data.description: Guide or control vehicular or pedestrian traffic at such places as streets, schools, railroad crossings, or construction sites

data.title: Lifeguards, Ski Patrol, and Other Recreational Protective Service Workers
data.description: Monitor recreational areas, such as pools, beaches, or ski slopes, to provide assistance and protection to participants

data.title: Transportation Security Screeners
data.description: Conduct screening of passengers, baggage, or cargo to ensure compliance with Transportation Security Administration (TSA) regulations. May operate basic security equipment such as x-ray machines and hand wands at screening checkpoints

data.title: School Bus Monitors
data.description: Maintain order among students on a school bus. Duties include helping students safely board and exit and communicating behavioral problems. May perform pretrip and posttrip inspections and prepare for and assist in emergency evacuations

data.title: Protective Service Workers, All Other
data.description: All protective service workers not listed separately

data.title: Retail Loss Prevention Specialists
data.description: Implement procedures and systems to prevent merchandise loss. Conduct audits and investigations of employee activity. May assist in developing policies, procedures, and systems for safeguarding assets

data.title: Chefs and Head Cooks
data.description: Direct and may participate in the preparation, seasoning, and cooking of salads, soups, fish, meats, vegetables, desserts, or other foods. May plan and price menu items, order supplies, and keep records and accounts

data.title: First-Line Supervisors of Food Preparation and Serving Workers
data.description: Directly supervise and coordinate activities of workers engaged in preparing and serving food

data.title: Cooks, Fast Food
data.description: Prepare and cook food in a fast food restaurant with a limited menu. Duties of these cooks are limited to preparation of a few basic items and normally involve operating large-volume single-purpose cooking equipment

data.title: Cooks, Institution and Cafeteria
data.description: Prepare and cook large quantities of food for institutions, such as schools, hospitals, or cafeterias

data.title: Cooks, Private Household
data.description: Prepare meals in private homes. Includes personal chefs

data.title: Cooks, Restaurant
data.description: Prepare, season, and cook dishes such as soups, meats, vegetables, or desserts in restaurants. May order supplies, keep records and accounts, price items on menu, or plan menu

data.title: Cooks, Short Order
data.description: Prepare and cook to order a variety of foods that require only a short preparation time. May take orders from customers and serve patrons at counters or tables

data.title: Cooks, All Other
data.description: All cooks not listed separately

data.title: Food Preparation Workers
data.description: Perform a variety of food preparation duties other than cooking, such as preparing cold foods and shellfish, slicing meat, and brewing coffee or tea

data.title: Bartenders
data.description: Mix and serve drinks to patrons, directly or through waitstaff

data.title: Fast Food and Counter Workers
data.description: Perform duties such as taking orders and serving food and beverages. Serve customers at counter or from a steam table. May take payment. May prepare food and beverages

data.title: Baristas
data.description: Prepare or serve specialty coffee or other beverages. Serve food such as baked goods or sandwiches to patrons

data.title: Waiters and Waitresses
data.description: Take orders and serve food and beverages to patrons at tables in dining establishment

data.title: Food Servers, Nonrestaurant
data.description: Serve food to individuals outside of a restaurant environment, such as in hotel rooms, hospital rooms, residential care facilities, or cars

data.title: Dining Room and Cafeteria Attendants and Bartender Helpers
data.description: Facilitate food service. Clean tables; remove dirty dishes; replace soiled table linens; set tables; replenish supply of clean linens, silverware, glassware, and dishes; supply service bar with food; and serve items such as water, condiments, and coffee to patrons

data.title: Dishwashers
data.description: Clean dishes, kitchen, food preparation equipment, or utensils

data.title: Hosts and Hostesses, Restaurant, Lounge, and Coffee Shop
data.description: Welcome patrons, seat them at tables or in lounge, and help ensure quality of facilities and service

data.title: Food Preparation and Serving Related Workers, All Other
data.description: All food preparation and serving related workers not listed separately

data.title: First-Line Supervisors of Housekeeping and Janitorial Workers
data.description: Directly supervise and coordinate work activities of cleaning personnel in hotels, hospitals, offices, and other establishments

data.title: First-Line Supervisors of Landscaping, Lawn Service, and Groundskeeping Workers
data.description: Directly supervise and coordinate activities of workers engaged in landscaping or groundskeeping activities. Work may involve reviewing contracts to ascertain service, machine, and workforce requirements; answering inquiries from potential customers regarding methods, material, and price ranges; and preparing estimates according to labor, material, and machine costs

data.title: Janitors and Cleaners, Except Maids and Housekeeping Cleaners
data.description: Keep buildings in clean and orderly condition. Perform heavy cleaning duties, such as cleaning floors, shampooing rugs, washing walls and glass, and removing rubbish. Duties may include tending furnace and boiler, performing routine maintenance activities, notifying management of need for repairs, and cleaning snow or debris from sidewalk

data.title: Maids and Housekeeping Cleaners
data.description: Perform any combination of light cleaning duties to maintain private households or commercial establishments, such as hotels and hospitals, in a clean and orderly manner. Duties may include making beds, replenishing linens, cleaning rooms and halls, and vacuuming

data.title: Building Cleaning Workers, All Other
data.description: All building cleaning workers not listed separately

data.title: Pest Control Workers
data.description: Apply or release chemical solutions or toxic gases and set traps to kill or remove pests and vermin that infest buildings and surrounding areas

data.title: Landscaping and Groundskeeping Workers
data.description: Landscape or maintain grounds of property using hand or power tools or equipment. Workers typically perform a variety of tasks, which may include any combination of the following: sod laying, mowing, trimming, planting, watering, fertilizing, digging, raking, sprinkler installation, and installation of mortarless segmental concrete masonry wall units

data.title: Pesticide Handlers, Sprayers, and Applicators, Vegetation
data.description: Mix or apply pesticides, herbicides, fungicides, or insecticides through sprays, dusts, vapors, soil incorporation, or chemical application on trees, shrubs, lawns, or crops. Usually requires specific training and state or federal certification

data.title: Tree Trimmers and Pruners
data.description: Using sophisticated climbing and rigging techniques, cut away dead or excess branches from trees or shrubs to maintain right-of-way for roads, sidewalks, or utilities, or to improve appearance, health, and value of tree. Prune or treat trees or shrubs using handsaws, hand pruners, clippers, and power pruners. Works off the ground in the tree canopy and may use truck-mounted lifts

data.title: Grounds Maintenance Workers, All Other
data.description: All grounds maintenance workers not listed separately

data.title: First-Line Supervisors of Gambling Services Workers
data.description: Directly supervise and coordinate activities of workers in assigned gambling areas. May circulate among tables, observe operations, and ensure that stations and games are covered for each shift. May verify and pay off jackpots. May reset slot machines after payoffs and make repairs or adjustments to slot machines or recommend removal of slot machines for repair. May plan and organize activities an

data.title: First-Line Supervisors of Entertainment and Recreation Workers, Except Gambling Services
data.description: Directly supervise and coordinate activities of entertainment and recreation related workers

data.title: First-Line Supervisors of Personal Service Workers
data.description: Supervise and coordinate activities of personal service workers

data.title: Animal Trainers
data.description: Train animals for riding, harness, security, performance, or obedience, or for assisting persons with disabilities. Accustom animals to human voice and contact, and condition animals to respond to commands. Train animals according to prescribed standards for show or competition. May train animals to carry pack loads or work as part of pack team

data.title: Animal Caretakers
data.description: Feed, water, groom, bathe, exercise, or otherwise provide care to promote and maintain the well-being of pets and other animals that are not raised for consumption, such as dogs, cats, race horses, ornamental fish or birds, zoo animals, and mice. Work in settings such as kennels, animal shelters, zoos, circuses, and aquariums. May keep records of feedings, treatments, and animals received or disch

data.title: Gambling Dealers
data.description: Operate table games. Stand or sit behind table and operate games of chance by dispensing the appropriate number of cards or blocks to players, or operating other gambling equipment. Distribute winnings or collect players' money or chips. May compare the house's hand against players' hands

data.title: Gambling and Sports Book Writers and Runners
data.description: Post information enabling patrons to wager on various races and sporting events. Assist in the operation of games such as keno and bingo. May operate random number-generating equipment and announce the numbers for patrons. Receive, verify, and record patrons' wagers. Scan and process winning tickets presented by patrons and pay out winnings for those wagers

data.title: Gambling Service Workers, All Other
data.description: All gambling service workers not listed separately

data.title: Motion Picture Projectionists
data.description: Set up and operate motion picture projection and related sound reproduction equipment

data.title: Ushers, Lobby Attendants, and Ticket Takers
data.description: Assist patrons at entertainment events by performing duties, such as collecting admission tickets and passes from patrons, assisting in finding seats, searching for lost articles, and helping patrons locate such facilities as restrooms and telephones

data.title: Amusement and Recreation Attendants
data.description: Perform a variety of attending duties at amusement or recreation facility. May schedule use of recreation facilities, maintain and provide equipment to participants of sporting events or recreational pursuits, or operate amusement concessions and rides

data.title: Costume Attendants
data.description: Select, fit, and take care of costumes for cast members, and aid entertainers. May assist with multiple costume changes during performances

data.title: Locker Room, Coatroom, and Dressing Room Attendants
data.description: Provide personal items to patrons or customers in locker rooms, dressing rooms, or coatrooms

data.title: Entertainment Attendants and Related Workers, All Other
data.description: All entertainment attendants and related workers not listed separately

data.title: Embalmers
data.description: Prepare bodies for interment in conformity with legal requirements

data.title: Crematory Operators
data.description: Operate crematory equipment to reduce human or animal remains to bone fragments in accordance with state and local regulations. Duties may include preparing the body for cremation and performing general maintenance on crematory equipment. May use traditional flame-based cremation, calcination, or alkaline hydrolysis

data.title: Funeral Attendants
data.description: Perform a variety of tasks during funeral, such as placing casket in parlor or chapel prior to service, arranging floral offerings or lights around casket, directing or escorting mourners, closing casket, and issuing and storing funeral equipment

data.title: Morticians, Undertakers, and Funeral Arrangers
data.description: Perform various tasks to arrange and direct individual funeral services, such as coordinating transportation of body to mortuary, interviewing family or other authorized person to arrange details, selecting pallbearers, aiding with the selection of officials for religious rites, and providing transportation for mourners

data.title: Barbers
data.description: Provide barbering services, such as cutting, trimming, shampooing, and styling hair; trimming beards; or giving shaves

data.title: Hairdressers, Hairstylists, and Cosmetologists
data.description: Provide beauty services, such as cutting, coloring, and styling hair, and massaging and treating scalp. May shampoo hair, apply makeup, dress wigs, remove hair, and provide nail and skincare services

data.title: Makeup Artists, Theatrical and Performance
data.description: Apply makeup to performers to reflect period, setting, and situation of their role
alternate_titles: Special Effects Makeup Artist (Special Effects MUA)
TV and Film Makeup Artist (Television and Film Makeup Artist)

data.title: Manicurists and Pedicurists
data.description: Clean and shape customers' fingernails and toenails. May polish or decorate nails

data.title: Shampooers
data.description: Shampoo and rinse customers' hair

data.title: Skincare Specialists
data.description: Provide skincare treatments to face and body to enhance an individual's appearance. Includes electrologists and laser hair removal specialists

data.title: Baggage Porters and Bellhops
data.description: Handle baggage for travelers at transportation terminals or for guests at hotels or similar establishments

data.title: Concierges
data.description: Assist patrons at hotel, apartment, or office building with personal services. May take messages; arrange or give advice on transportation, business services, or entertainment; or monitor guest requests for housekeeping and maintenance

data.title: Tour Guides and Escorts
data.description: Escort individuals or groups on sightseeing tours or through places of interest, such as industrial establishments, public buildings, and art galleries

data.title: Travel Guides
data.description: Plan, organize, and conduct long-distance travel, tours, and expeditions for individuals and groups

data.title: Childcare Workers
data.description: Attend to children at schools, businesses, private households, and childcare institutions. Perform a variety of tasks, such as dressing, feeding, bathing, and overseeing play

data.title: Nannies
data.description: Care for children in private households and provide support and expertise to parents in satisfying children's physical, emotional, intellectual, and social needs. Duties may include meal planning and preparation, laundry and clothing care, organization of play activities and outings, discipline, intellectual stimulation, language activities, and transportation

data.title: Exercise Trainers and Group Fitness Instructors
data.description: Instruct or coach groups or individuals in exercise activities for the primary purpose of personal fitness. Demonstrate techniques and form, observe participants, and explain to them corrective measures necessary to improve their skills. Develop and implement individualized approaches to exercise

data.title: Recreation Workers
data.description: Conduct recreation activities with groups in public, private, or volunteer agencies or recreation facilities. Organize and promote activities, such as arts and crafts, sports, games, music, dramatics, social recreation, camping, and hobbies, taking into account the needs and interests of individual members

data.title: Residential Advisors
data.description: Coordinate activities in resident facilities in secondary school and college dormitories, group homes, or similar establishments. Order supplies and determine need for maintenance, repairs, and furnishings. May maintain household records and assign rooms. May assist residents with problem solving or refer them to counseling resources

data.title: Personal Care and Service Workers, All Other
data.description: All personal care and service workers not listed separately

data.title: First-Line Supervisors of Retail Sales Workers
data.description: Directly supervise and coordinate activities of retail sales workers in an establishment or department. Duties may include management functions, such as purchasing, budgeting, accounting, and personnel work, in addition to supervisory duties

data.title: First-Line Supervisors of Non-Retail Sales Workers
data.description: Directly supervise and coordinate activities of sales workers other than retail sales workers. May perform duties such as budgeting, accounting, and personnel work, in addition to supervisory duties

data.title: Cashiers
data.description: Receive and disburse money in establishments other than financial institutions. May use electronic scanners, cash registers, or related equipment. May process credit or debit card transactions and validate checks

data.title: Gambling Change Persons and Booth Cashiers
data.description: Exchange coins, tokens, and chips for patrons' money. May issue payoffs and obtain customer's signature on receipt. May operate a booth in the slot machine area and furnish change persons with money bank at the start of the shift, or count and audit money in drawers

data.title: Counter and Rental Clerks
data.description: Receive orders, generally in person, for repairs, rentals, and services. May describe available options, compute cost, and accept payment

data.title: Parts Salespersons
data.description: Sell spare and replacement parts and equipment in repair shop or parts store

data.title: Retail Salespersons
data.description: Sell merchandise, such as furniture, motor vehicles, appliances, or apparel to consumers

data.title: Advertising Sales Agents
data.description: Sell or solicit advertising space, time, or media in publications, signage, TV, radio, or Internet establishments or public spaces

data.title: Insurance Sales Agents
data.description: Sell life, property, casualty, health, automotive, or other types of insurance. May refer clients to independent brokers, work as an independent broker, or be employed by an insurance company

data.title: Securities, Commodities, and Financial Services Sales Agents
data.description: Buy and sell securities or commodities in investment and trading firms, or provide financial services to businesses and individuals. May advise customers about stocks, bonds, mutual funds, commodities, and market conditions

data.title: Travel Agents
data.description: Plan and sell transportation and accommodations for customers. Determine destination, modes of transportation, travel dates, costs, and accommodations required. May also describe, plan, and arrange itineraries and sell tour packages. May assist in resolving clients' travel problems

data.title: Sales Representatives of Services, Except Advertising, Insurance, Financial Services, and Travel
data.description: Sell services to individuals or businesses. May describe options or resolve client problems

data.title: Sales Representatives, Wholesale and Manufacturing, Technical and Scientific Products
data.description: Sell goods for wholesalers or manufacturers where technical or scientific knowledge is required in such areas as biology, engineering, chemistry, and electronics, normally obtained from at least 2 years of postsecondary education

data.title: Solar Sales Representatives and Assessors
data.description: Contact new or existing customers to determine their solar equipment needs, suggest systems or equipment, or estimate costs

data.title: Sales Representatives, Wholesale and Manufacturing, Except Technical and Scientific Products
data.description: Sell goods for wholesalers or manufacturers to businesses or groups of individuals. Work requires substantial knowledge of items sold

data.title: Demonstrators and Product Promoters
data.description: Demonstrate merchandise and answer questions for the purpose of creating public interest in buying the product. May sell demonstrated merchandise

data.title: Models
data.description: Model garments or other apparel and accessories for prospective buyers at fashion shows, private showings, or retail establishments. May pose for photos to be used in magazines or advertisements. May pose as subject for paintings, sculptures, and other types of artistic expression

data.title: Real Estate Brokers
data.description: Operate real estate office, or work for commercial real estate firm, overseeing real estate transactions. Other duties usually include selling real estate or renting properties and arranging loans

data.title: Real Estate Sales Agents
data.description: Rent, buy, or sell property for clients. Perform duties such as study property listings, interview prospective clients, accompany clients to property site, discuss conditions of sale, and draw up real estate contracts. Includes agents who represent buyer

data.title: Sales Engineers
data.description: Sell business goods or services, the selling of which requires a technical background equivalent to a baccalaureate degree in engineering

data.title: Telemarketers
data.description: Solicit donations or orders for goods or services over the telephone

data.title: Door-to-Door Sales Workers, News and Street Vendors, and Related Workers
data.description: Sell goods or services door-to-door or on the street

data.title: Sales and Related Workers, All Other
data.description: All sales and related workers not listed separately

data.title: First-Line Supervisors of Office and Administrative Support Workers
data.description: Directly supervise and coordinate the activities of clerical and administrative support workers

data.title: Switchboard Operators, Including Answering Service
data.description: Operate telephone business systems equipment or switchboards to relay incoming, outgoing, and interoffice calls. May supply information to callers and record messages

data.title: Telephone Operators
data.description: Provide information by accessing alphabetical, geographical, or other directories. Assist customers with special billing requests, such as charges to a third party and credits or refunds for incorrectly dialed numbers or bad connections. May handle emergency calls and assist children or people with physical disabilities to make telephone calls
alternate_titles: 411 Directory Assistance Operator (411 Directory Assistance Op)

data.title: Communications Equipment Operators, All Other
data.description: All communications equipment operators not listed separately

data.title: Bill and Account Collectors
data.description: Locate and notify customers of delinquent accounts by mail, telephone, or personal visit to solicit payment. Duties include receiving payment and posting amount to customer's account, preparing statements to credit department if customer fails to respond, initiating repossession proceedings or service disconnection, and keeping records of collection and status of accounts

data.title: Billing and Posting Clerks
data.description: Compile, compute, and record billing, accounting, statistical, and other numerical data for billing purposes. Prepare billing invoices for services rendered or for delivery or shipment of goods

data.title: Bookkeeping, Accounting, and Auditing Clerks
data.description: Compute, classify, and record numerical data to keep financial records complete. Perform any combination of routine calculating, posting, and verifying duties to obtain primary financial data for use in maintaining accounting records. May also check the accuracy of figures, calculations, and postings pertaining to business transactions recorded by other workers

data.title: Gambling Cage Workers
data.description: In a gambling establishment, conduct financial transactions for patrons. Accept patron's credit application and verify credit references to provide check-cashing authorization or to establish house credit accounts. May reconcile daily summaries of transactions to balance books. May sell gambling chips, tokens, or tickets to patrons, or to other workers for resale to patrons. May convert gambling c
alternate_titles: Cage and Players Club Rep (Cage and Players Club Representative)

data.title: Payroll and Timekeeping Clerks
data.description: Compile and record employee time and payroll data. May compute employees' time worked, production, and commission. May compute and post wages and deductions, or prepare paychecks

data.title: Procurement Clerks
data.description: Compile information and records to draw up purchase orders for procurement of materials and services

data.title: Tellers
data.description: Receive and pay out money. Keep records of money and negotiable instruments involved in a financial institution's various transactions

data.title: Financial Clerks, All Other
data.description: All financial clerks not listed separately

data.title: Brokerage Clerks
data.description: Perform duties related to the purchase, sale, or holding of securities. Duties include writing orders for stock purchases or sales, computing transfer taxes, verifying stock transactions, accepting and delivering securities, tracking stock price fluctuations, computing equity, distributing dividends, and keeping records of daily transactions and holdings

data.title: Correspondence Clerks
data.description: Compose letters or electronic correspondence in reply to requests for merchandise, damage claims, credit and other information, delinquent accounts, incorrect billings, or unsatisfactory services. Duties may include gathering data to formulate reply and preparing correspondence

data.title: Court, Municipal, and License Clerks
data.description: Perform clerical duties for courts of law, municipalities, or governmental licensing agencies and bureaus. May prepare docket of cases to be called; secure information for judges and court; prepare draft agendas or bylaws for town or city council; answer official correspondence; keep fiscal records and accounts; issue licenses or permits; and record data, administer tests, or collect fees

data.title: Credit Authorizers, Checkers, and Clerks
data.description: Authorize credit charges against customers' accounts. Investigate history and credit standing of individuals or business establishments applying for credit. May interview applicants to obtain personal and financial data, determine credit worthiness, process applications, and notify customers of acceptance or rejection of credit

data.title: Customer Service Representatives
data.description: Interact with customers to provide basic or scripted information in response to routine inquiries about products and services. May handle and resolve general complaints. Excludes individuals whose duties are primarily installation, sales, repair, and technical support

data.title: Eligibility Interviewers, Government Programs
data.description: Determine eligibility of persons applying to receive assistance from government programs and agency resources, such as welfare, unemployment benefits, social security, and public housing

data.title: File Clerks
data.description: File correspondence, cards, invoices, receipts, and other records in alphabetical or numerical order or according to the filing system used. Locate and remove material from file when requested

data.title: Hotel, Motel, and Resort Desk Clerks
data.description: Accommodate hotel, motel, and resort patrons by registering and assigning rooms to guests, issuing room keys or cards, transmitting and receiving messages, keeping records of occupied rooms and guests' accounts, making and confirming reservations, and presenting statements to and collecting payments from departing guests

data.title: Interviewers, Except Eligibility and Loan
data.description: Interview persons by telephone, mail, in person, or by other means for the purpose of completing forms, applications, or questionnaires. Ask specific questions, record answers, and assist persons with completing form. May sort, classify, and file forms

data.title: Library Assistants, Clerical
data.description: Compile records, and sort, shelve, issue, and receive library materials such as books, electronic media, pictures, cards, slides and microfilm. Locate library materials for loan and replace material in shelving area, stacks, or files according to identification number and title. Register patrons to permit them to borrow books, periodicals, and other library materials

data.title: Loan Interviewers and Clerks
data.description: Interview loan applicants to elicit information; investigate applicants' backgrounds and verify references; prepare loan request papers; and forward findings, reports, and documents to appraisal department. Review loan papers to ensure completeness, and complete transactions between loan establishment, borrowers, and sellers upon approval of loan

data.title: New Accounts Clerks
data.description: Interview persons desiring to open accounts in financial institutions. Explain account services available to prospective customers and assist them in preparing applications

data.title: Order Clerks
data.description: Receive and process incoming orders for materials, merchandise, classified ads, or services such as repairs, installations, or rental of facilities. Generally receives orders via mail, phone, fax, or other electronic means. Duties include informing customers of receipt, prices, shipping dates, and delays; preparing contracts; and handling complaints

data.title: Human Resources Assistants, Except Payroll and Timekeeping
data.description: Compile and keep personnel records. Record data for each employee, such as address, weekly earnings, absences, amount of sales or production, supervisory reports, and date of and reason for termination. May prepare reports for employment records, file employment records, or search employee files and furnish information to authorized persons
alternate_titles: Human Resources Administrative Assistant (HR Administrative Assistant)

data.title: Receptionists and Information Clerks
data.description: Answer inquiries and provide information to the general public, customers, visitors, and other interested parties regarding activities conducted at establishment and location of departments, offices, and employees within the organization

data.title: Reservation and Transportation Ticket Agents and Travel Clerks
data.description: Make and confirm reservations for transportation or lodging, or sell transportation tickets. May check baggage and direct passengers to designated concourse, pier, or track; deliver tickets and contact individuals and groups to inform them of package tours; or provide tourists with travel or transportation information

data.title: Information and Record Clerks, All Other
data.description: All information and record clerks not listed separately

data.title: Cargo and Freight Agents
data.description: Expedite and route movement of incoming and outgoing cargo and freight shipments in airline, train, and trucking terminals and shipping docks. Take orders from customers and arrange pickup of freight and cargo for delivery to loading platform. Prepare and examine bills of lading to determine shipping charges and tariffs

data.title: Freight Forwarders
data.description: Research rates, routings, or modes of transport for shipment of products. Maintain awareness of regulations affecting the international movement of cargo. Make arrangements for additional services, such as storage or inland transportation

data.title: Couriers and Messengers
data.description: Pick up and deliver messages, documents, packages, and other items between offices or departments within an establishment or directly to other business concerns, traveling by foot, bicycle, motorcycle, automobile, or public conveyance

data.title: Public Safety Telecommunicators
data.description: Operate telephone, radio, or other communication systems to receive and communicate requests for emergency assistance at 9-1-1 public safety answering points and emergency operations centers. Take information from the public and other sources regarding crimes, threats, disturbances, acts of terrorism, fires, medical emergencies, and other public safety matters. May coordinate and provide informati

data.title: Dispatchers, Except Police, Fire, and Ambulance
data.description: Schedule and dispatch workers, work crews, equipment, or service vehicles for conveyance of materials, freight, or passengers, or for normal installation, service, or emergency repairs rendered outside the place of business. Duties may include using radio, telephone, or computer to transmit assignments and compiling statistics and reports on work progress

data.title: Meter Readers, Utilities
data.description: Read meter and record consumption of electricity, gas, water, or steam

data.title: Postal Service Clerks
data.description: Perform any combination of tasks in a United States Postal Service (USPS) post office, such as receive letters and parcels; sell postage and revenue stamps, postal cards, and stamped envelopes; fill out and sell money orders; place mail in pigeon holes of mail rack or in bags; and examine mail for correct postage. Includes postal service clerks employed by USPS contractors

data.title: Postal Service Mail Carriers
data.description: Sort and deliver mail for the United States Postal Service (USPS). Deliver mail on established route by vehicle or on foot. Includes postal service mail carriers employed by USPS contractors

data.title: Postal Service Mail Sorters, Processors, and Processing Machine Operators
data.description: Prepare incoming and outgoing mail for distribution for the United States Postal Service (USPS). Examine, sort, and route mail. Load, operate, and occasionally adjust and repair mail processing, sorting, and canceling machinery. Keep records of shipments, pouches, and sacks, and perform other duties related to mail handling within the postal service. Includes postal service mail sorters and proces
alternate_titles: Computer Forwarding System Markup Clerk (CFS Markup Clerk)
Small Package and Bundle Sorter Clerk (SPBS Clerk)

data.title: Production, Planning, and Expediting Clerks
data.description: Coordinate and expedite the flow of work and materials within or between departments of an establishment according to production schedule. Duties include reviewing and distributing production, work, and shipment schedules; conferring with department supervisors to determine progress of work and completion dates; and compiling reports on progress of work, inventory levels, costs, and production pro

data.title: Shipping, Receiving, and Inventory Clerks
data.description: Verify and maintain records on incoming and outgoing shipments involving inventory. Duties include verifying and recording incoming merchandise or material and arranging for the transportation of products. May prepare items for shipment

data.title: Weighers, Measurers, Checkers, and Samplers, Recordkeeping
data.description: Weigh, measure, and check materials, supplies, and equipment for the purpose of keeping relevant records. Duties are primarily clerical by nature. Includes workers who collect and keep record of samples of products or materials

data.title: Executive Secretaries and Executive Administrative Assistants
data.description: Provide high-level administrative support by conducting research, preparing statistical reports, and handling information requests, as well as performing routine administrative functions such as preparing correspondence, receiving visitors, arranging conference calls, and scheduling meetings. May also train and supervise lower-level clerical staff

data.title: Legal Secretaries and Administrative Assistants
data.description: Perform secretarial duties using legal terminology, procedures, and documents. Prepare legal papers and correspondence, such as summonses, complaints, motions, and subpoenas. May also assist with legal research

data.title: Medical Secretaries and Administrative Assistants
data.description: Perform secretarial duties using specific knowledge of medical terminology and hospital, clinic, or laboratory procedures. Duties may include scheduling appointments, billing patients, and compiling and recording medical charts, reports, and correspondence

data.title: Secretaries and Administrative Assistants, Except Legal, Medical, and Executive
data.description: Perform routine administrative functions such as drafting correspondence, scheduling appointments, organizing and maintaining paper and electronic files, or providing information to callers

data.title: Data Entry Keyers
data.description: Operate data entry device, such as keyboard or photo composing perforator. Duties may include verifying data and preparing materials for printing

data.title: Word Processors and Typists
data.description: Use word processor, computer, or typewriter to type letters, reports, forms, or other material from rough draft, corrected copy, or voice recording. May perform other clerical duties as assigned

data.title: Desktop Publishers
data.description: Format typescript and graphic elements using computer software to produce publication-ready material

data.title: Insurance Claims and Policy Processing Clerks
data.description: Process new insurance policies, modifications to existing policies, and claims forms. Obtain information from policyholders to verify the accuracy and completeness of information on claims forms, applications and related documents, and company records. Update existing policies and company records to reflect changes requested by policyholders and insurance company representatives

data.title: Mail Clerks and Mail Machine Operators, Except Postal Service
data.description: Prepare incoming and outgoing mail for distribution. Time-stamp, open, read, sort, and route incoming mail; and address, seal, stamp, fold, stuff, and affix postage to outgoing mail or packages. Duties may also include keeping necessary records and completed forms

data.title: Office Clerks, General
data.description: Perform duties too varied and diverse to be classified in any specific office clerical occupation, requiring knowledge of office systems and procedures. Clerical duties may be assigned in accordance with the office procedures of individual establishments and may include a combination of answering telephones, bookkeeping, typing or word processing, office machine operation, and filing

data.title: Office Machine Operators, Except Computer
data.description: Operate one or more of a variety of office machines, such as photocopying, photographic, and duplicating machines, or other office machines

data.title: Proofreaders and Copy Markers
data.description: Read transcript or proof type setup to detect and mark for correction any grammatical, typographical, or compositional errors. Excludes workers whose primary duty is editing copy. Includes proofreaders of braille

data.title: Statistical Assistants
data.description: Compile and compute data according to statistical formulas for use in statistical studies. May perform actuarial computations and compile charts and graphs for use by actuaries. Includes actuarial clerks

data.title: Office and Administrative Support Workers, All Other
data.description: All office and administrative support workers not listed separately

data.title: First-Line Supervisors of Farming, Fishing, and Forestry Workers
data.description: Directly supervise and coordinate the activities of agricultural, forestry, aquacultural, and related workers

data.title: Agricultural Inspectors
data.description: Inspect agricultural commodities, processing equipment, and facilities, and fish and logging operations, to ensure compliance with regulations and laws governing health, quality, and safety
alternate_titles: Food Safety and Inspection Service Inspector (FSIS Inspector)

data.title: Animal Breeders
data.description: Select and breed animals according to their genealogy, characteristics, and offspring. May require knowledge of artificial insemination techniques and equipment use. May involve keeping records on heats, birth intervals, or pedigree

data.title: Graders and Sorters, Agricultural Products
data.description: Grade, sort, or classify unprocessed food and other agricultural products by size, weight, color, or condition

data.title: Agricultural Equipment Operators
data.description: Drive and control equipment to support agricultural activities such as tilling soil; planting, cultivating, and harvesting crops; feeding and herding livestock; or removing animal waste. May perform tasks such as crop baling or hay bucking. May operate stationary equipment to perform post-harvest tasks such as husking, shelling, threshing, and ginning

data.title: Farmworkers and Laborers, Crop, Nursery, and Greenhouse
data.description: Manually plant, cultivate, and harvest vegetables, fruits, nuts, horticultural specialties, and field crops. Use hand tools, such as shovels, trowels, hoes, tampers, pruning hooks, shears, and knives. Duties may include tilling soil and applying fertilizers; transplanting, weeding, thinning, or pruning crops; applying pesticides; or cleaning, grading, sorting, packing, and loading harvested produc

data.title: Farmworkers, Farm, Ranch, and Aquacultural Animals
data.description: Attend to live farm, ranch, open range or aquacultural animals that may include cattle, sheep, swine, goats, horses and other equines, poultry, rabbits, finfish, shellfish, and bees. Attend to animals produced for animal products, such as meat, fur, skins, feathers, eggs, milk, and honey. Duties may include feeding, watering, herding, grazing, milking, castrating, branding, de-beaking, weighing, c

data.title: Agricultural Workers, All Other
data.description: All agricultural workers not listed separately

data.title: Fishing and Hunting Workers
data.description: Hunt, trap, catch, or gather wild animals or aquatic animals and plants. May use nets, traps, or other equipment. May haul catch onto ship or other vessel

data.title: Forest and Conservation Workers
data.description: Under supervision, perform manual labor necessary to develop, maintain, or protect areas such as forests, forested areas, woodlands, wetlands, and rangelands through such activities as raising and transporting seedlings; combating insects, pests, and diseases harmful to plant life; and building structures to control water, erosion, and leaching of soil. Includes forester aides, seedling pullers, t

data.title: Fallers
data.description: Use axes or chainsaws to fell trees using knowledge of tree characteristics and cutting techniques to control direction of fall and minimize tree damage

data.title: Logging Equipment Operators
data.description: Drive logging tractor or wheeled vehicle equipped with one or more accessories, such as bulldozer blade, frontal shear, grapple, logging arch, cable winches, hoisting rack, or crane boom, to fell tree; to skid, load, unload, or stack logs; or to pull stumps or clear brush. Includes operating stand-alone logging machines, such as log chippers

data.title: Log Graders and Scalers
data.description: Grade logs or estimate the marketable content or value of logs or pulpwood in sorting yards, millpond, log deck, or similar locations. Inspect logs for defects or measure logs to determine volume

data.title: Logging Workers, All Other
data.description: All logging workers not listed separately

data.title: First-Line Supervisors of Construction Trades and Extraction Workers
data.description: Directly supervise and coordinate activities of construction or extraction workers

data.title: Solar Energy Installation Managers
data.description: Direct work crews installing residential or commercial solar photovoltaic or thermal systems

data.title: Boilermakers
data.description: Construct, assemble, maintain, and repair stationary steam boilers and boiler house auxiliaries. Align structures or plate sections to assemble boiler frame tanks or vats, following blueprints. Work involves use of hand and power tools, plumb bobs, levels, wedges, dogs, or turnbuckles. Assist in testing assembled vessels. Direct cleaning of boilers and boiler furnaces. Inspect and repair boiler fi
alternate_titles: Industrial Boiler Service Technician (Industrial Boiler Service Tech)

data.title: Brickmasons and Blockmasons
data.description: Lay and bind building materials, such as brick, structural tile, concrete block, cinder block, glass block, and terra-cotta block, with mortar and other substances, to construct or repair walls, partitions, arches, sewers, and other structures

data.title: Stonemasons
data.description: Build stone structures, such as piers, walls, and abutments. Lay walks, curbstones, or special types of masonry for vats, tanks, and floors

data.title: Carpenters
data.description: Construct, erect, install, or repair structures and fixtures made of wood and comparable materials, such as concrete forms; building frameworks, including partitions, joists, studding, and rafters; and wood stairways, window and door frames, and hardwood floors. May also install cabinets, siding, drywall, and batt or roll insulation. Includes brattice builders who build doors or brattices (ventila

data.title: Carpet Installers
data.description: Lay and install carpet from rolls or blocks on floors. Install padding and trim flooring materials

data.title: Floor Layers, Except Carpet, Wood, and Hard Tiles
data.description: Apply blocks, strips, or sheets of shock-absorbing, sound-deadening, or decorative coverings to floors

data.title: Floor Sanders and Finishers
data.description: Scrape and sand wooden floors to smooth surfaces using floor scraper and floor sanding machine, and apply coats of finish

data.title: Tile and Stone Setters
data.description: Apply hard tile, stone, and comparable materials to walls, floors, ceilings, countertops, and roof decks

data.title: Cement Masons and Concrete Finishers
data.description: Smooth and finish surfaces of poured concrete, such as floors, walks, sidewalks, roads, or curbs using a variety of hand and power tools. Align forms for sidewalks, curbs, or gutters; patch voids; and use saws to cut expansion joints

data.title: Terrazzo Workers and Finishers
data.description: Apply a mixture of cement, sand, pigment, or marble chips to floors, stairways, and cabinet fixtures to fashion durable and decorative surfaces

data.title: Construction Laborers
data.description: Perform tasks involving physical labor at construction sites. May operate hand and power tools of all types: air hammers, earth tampers, cement mixers, small mechanical hoists, surveying and measuring equipment, and a variety of other equipment and instruments. May clean and prepare sites, dig trenches, set braces to support the sides of excavations, erect scaffolding, and clean up rubble, debris,

data.title: Paving, Surfacing, and Tamping Equipment Operators
data.description: Operate equipment used for applying concrete, asphalt, or other materials to road beds, parking lots, or airport runways and taxiways or for tamping gravel, dirt, or other materials. Includes concrete and asphalt paving machine operators, form tampers, tamping machine operators, and stone spreader operators

data.title: Pile Driver Operators
data.description: Operate pile drivers mounted on skids, barges, crawler treads, or locomotive cranes to drive pilings for retaining walls, bulkheads, and foundations of structures such as buildings, bridges, and piers

data.title: Operating Engineers and Other Construction Equipment Operators
data.description: Operate one or several types of power construction equipment, such as motor graders, bulldozers, scrapers, compressors, pumps, derricks, shovels, tractors, or front-end loaders to excavate, move, and grade earth, erect structures, or pour concrete or other hard surface pavement. May repair and maintain equipment in addition to other duties

data.title: Drywall and Ceiling Tile Installers
data.description: Apply plasterboard or other wallboard to ceilings or interior walls of buildings. Apply or mount acoustical tiles or blocks, strips, or sheets of shock-absorbing materials to ceilings and walls of buildings to reduce or reflect sound. Materials may be of decorative quality. Includes lathers who fasten wooden, metal, or rockboard lath to walls, ceilings, or partitions of buildings to provide suppor

data.title: Tapers
data.description: Seal joints between plasterboard or other wallboard to prepare wall surface for painting or papering

data.title: Electricians
data.description: Install, maintain, and repair electrical wiring, equipment, and fixtures. Ensure that work is in accordance with relevant codes. May install or service street lights, intercom systems, or electrical control systems

data.title: Glaziers
data.description: Install glass in windows, skylights, store fronts, and display cases, or on surfaces, such as building fronts, interior walls, ceilings, and tabletops

data.title: Insulation Workers, Floor, Ceiling, and Wall
data.description: Line and cover structures with insulating materials. May work with batt, roll, or blown insulation materials

data.title: Insulation Workers, Mechanical
data.description: Apply insulating materials to pipes or ductwork, or other mechanical systems in order to help control and maintain temperature

data.title: Painters, Construction and Maintenance
data.description: Paint walls, equipment, buildings, bridges, and other structural surfaces, using brushes, rollers, and spray guns. May remove old paint to prepare surface prior to painting. May mix colors or oils to obtain desired color or consistency

data.title: Paperhangers
data.description: Cover interior walls or ceilings of rooms with decorative wallpaper or fabric, or attach advertising posters on surfaces such as walls and billboards. May remove old materials or prepare surfaces to be papered

data.title: Pipelayers
data.description: Lay pipe for storm or sanitation sewers, drains, and water mains. Perform any combination of the following tasks: grade trenches or culverts, position pipe, or seal joints

data.title: Plumbers, Pipefitters, and Steamfitters
data.description: Assemble, install, alter, and repair pipelines or pipe systems that carry water, steam, air, or other liquids or gases. May install heating and cooling equipment and mechanical control systems. Includes sprinkler fitters

data.title: Solar Thermal Installers and Technicians
data.description: Install or repair solar energy systems designed to collect, store, and circulate solar-heated water for residential, commercial or industrial use

data.title: Plasterers and Stucco Masons
data.description: Apply interior or exterior plaster, cement, stucco, or similar materials. May also set ornamental plaster

data.title: Reinforcing Iron and Rebar Workers
data.description: Position and secure steel bars or mesh in concrete forms in order to reinforce concrete. Use a variety of fasteners, rod-bending machines, blowtorches, and hand tools. Includes rod busters

data.title: Roofers
data.description: Cover roofs of structures with shingles, slate, asphalt, aluminum, wood, or related materials. May spray roofs, sidings, and walls with material to bind, seal, insulate, or soundproof sections of structures

data.title: Sheet Metal Workers
data.description: Fabricate, assemble, install, and repair sheet metal products and equipment, such as ducts, control boxes, drainpipes, and furnace casings. Work may involve any of the following: setting up and operating fabricating machines to cut, bend, and straighten sheet metal; shaping metal over anvils, blocks, or forms using hammer; operating soldering and welding equipment to join sheet metal parts; or ins
alternate_titles: HVAC Sheet Metal Installer (Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning Sheet Metal Installer)
HVAC Sheet Metal Specialist (Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning Sheet Metal Specialist)

data.title: Structural Iron and Steel Workers
data.description: Raise, place, and unite iron or steel girders, columns, and other structural members to form completed structures or structural frameworks. May erect metal storage tanks and assemble prefabricated metal buildings

data.title: Solar Photovoltaic Installers
data.description: Assemble, install, or maintain solar photovoltaic (PV) systems on roofs or other structures in compliance with site assessment and schematics. May include measuring, cutting, assembling, and bolting structural framing and solar modules. May perform minor electrical work such as current checks
alternate_titles: Journeyman Electrician PV Installer (Journeyman Electrician Photovoltaic Installer)
Solar Panel Installation Technician (Solar Panel Installation Tech)

data.title: Helpers--Brickmasons, Blockmasons, Stonemasons, and Tile and Marble Setters
data.description: Help brickmasons, blockmasons, stonemasons, or tile and marble setters by performing duties requiring less skill. Duties include using, supplying, or holding materials or tools, and cleaning work area and equipment

data.title: Helpers--Carpenters
data.description: Help carpenters by performing duties requiring less skill. Duties include using, supplying, or holding materials or tools, and cleaning work area and equipment

data.title: Helpers--Electricians
data.description: Help electricians by performing duties requiring less skill. Duties include using, supplying, or holding materials or tools, and cleaning work area and equipment
alternate_titles: E and I Apprentice (Electrical and Instrumentation Apprentice)
E and I Apprentice (Electrician and Instrumentation Apprentice)

data.title: Helpers--Painters, Paperhangers, Plasterers, and Stucco Masons
data.description: Help painters, paperhangers, plasterers, or stucco masons by performing duties requiring less skill. Duties include using, supplying, or holding materials or tools, and cleaning work area and equipment

data.title: Helpers--Pipelayers, Plumbers, Pipefitters, and Steamfitters
data.description: Help plumbers, pipefitters, steamfitters, or pipelayers by performing duties requiring less skill. Duties include using, supplying, or holding materials or tools, and cleaning work area and equipment

data.title: Helpers--Roofers
data.description: Help roofers by performing duties requiring less skill. Duties include using, supplying, or holding materials or tools, and cleaning work area and equipment

data.title: Helpers, Construction Trades, All Other
data.description: All construction trades helpers not listed separately

data.title: Construction and Building Inspectors
data.description: Inspect structures using engineering skills to determine structural soundness and compliance with specifications, building codes, and other regulations. Inspections may be general in nature or may be limited to a specific area, such as electrical systems or plumbing

data.title: Energy Auditors
data.description: Conduct energy audits of buildings, building systems, or process systems. May also conduct investment grade audits of buildings or systems

data.title: Elevator and Escalator Installers and Repairers
data.description: Assemble, install, repair, or maintain electric or hydraulic freight or passenger elevators, escalators, or dumbwaiters
alternate_titles: Elevator Repair and Maintenance Technician (Elevator Repair and Maintenance Tech)

data.title: Fence Erectors
data.description: Erect and repair fences and fence gates, using hand and power tools

data.title: Hazardous Materials Removal Workers
data.description: Identify, remove, pack, transport, or dispose of hazardous materials, including asbestos, lead-based paint, waste oil, fuel, transmission fluid, radioactive materials, or contaminated soil. Specialized training and certification in hazardous materials handling or a confined entry permit are generally required. May operate earth-moving equipment or trucks
alternate_titles: Decontamination and Decommissioning Operator (D and D Operator)

data.title: Highway Maintenance Workers
data.description: Maintain highways, municipal and rural roads, airport runways, and rights-of-way. Duties include patching broken or eroded pavement and repairing guard rails, highway markers, and snow fences. May also mow or clear brush from along road, or plow snow from roadway

data.title: Rail-Track Laying and Maintenance Equipment Operators
data.description: Lay, repair, and maintain track for standard or narrow-gauge railroad equipment used in regular railroad service or in plant yards, quarries, sand and gravel pits, and mines. Includes ballast cleaning machine operators and railroad bed tamping machine operators

data.title: Septic Tank Servicers and Sewer Pipe Cleaners
data.description: Clean and repair septic tanks, sewer lines, or drains. May patch walls and partitions of tank, replace damaged drain tile, or repair breaks in underground piping

data.title: Segmental Pavers
data.description: Lay out, cut, and place segmental paving units. Includes installers of bedding and restraining materials for the paving units

data.title: Construction and Related Workers, All Other
data.description: All construction and related workers not listed separately

data.title: Weatherization Installers and Technicians
data.description: Perform a variety of activities to weatherize homes and make them more energy efficient. Duties include repairing windows, insulating ducts, and performing heating, ventilating, and air-conditioning (HVAC) work. May perform energy audits and advise clients on energy conservation measures

data.title: Derrick Operators, Oil and Gas
data.description: Rig derrick equipment and operate pumps to circulate mud or fluid through drill hole

data.title: Rotary Drill Operators, Oil and Gas
data.description: Set up or operate a variety of drills to remove underground oil and gas, or remove core samples for testing during oil and gas exploration

data.title: Service Unit Operators, Oil and Gas
data.description: Operate equipment to increase oil flow from producing wells or to remove stuck pipe, casing, tools, or other obstructions from drilling wells. Includes fishing-tool technicians

data.title: Excavating and Loading Machine and Dragline Operators, Surface Mining
data.description: Operate or tend machinery at surface mining site, equipped with scoops, shovels, or buckets to excavate and load loose materials

data.title: Earth Drillers, Except Oil and Gas
data.description: Operate a variety of drills such as rotary, churn, and pneumatic to tap subsurface water and salt deposits, to remove core samples during mineral exploration or soil testing, and to facilitate the use of explosives in mining or construction. Includes horizontal and earth boring machine operators

data.title: Explosives Workers, Ordnance Handling Experts, and Blasters
data.description: Place and detonate explosives to demolish structures or to loosen, remove, or displace earth, rock, or other materials. May perform specialized handling, storage, and accounting procedures

data.title: Continuous Mining Machine Operators
data.description: Operate self-propelled mining machines that rip coal, metal and nonmetal ores, rock, stone, or sand from the mine face and load it onto conveyors, shuttle cars, or trucks in a continuous operation

data.title: Roof Bolters, Mining
data.description: Operate machinery to install roof support bolts in underground mine

data.title: Loading and Moving Machine Operators, Underground Mining
data.description: Operate underground loading or moving machine to load or move coal, ore, or rock using shuttle or mine car or conveyors. Equipment may include power shovels, hoisting engines equipped with cable-drawn scraper or scoop, or machines equipped with gathering arms and conveyor

data.title: Underground Mining Machine Operators, All Other
data.description: All underground mining machine operators not listed separately

data.title: Rock Splitters, Quarry
data.description: Separate blocks of rough dimension stone from quarry mass using jackhammers, wedges, or chop saws

data.title: Roustabouts, Oil and Gas
data.description: Assemble or repair oil field equipment using hand and power tools. Perform other tasks as needed

data.title: Helpers--Extraction Workers
data.description: Help extraction craft workers, such as earth drillers, blasters and explosives workers, derrick operators, and mining machine operators, by performing duties requiring less skill. Duties include supplying equipment or cleaning work area

data.title: Extraction Workers, All Other
data.description: All extraction workers not listed separately

data.title: First-Line Supervisors of Mechanics, Installers, and Repairers
data.description: Directly supervise and coordinate the activities of mechanics, installers, and repairers. May also advise customers on recommended services. Excludes team or work leaders
alternate_titles: Electrical and Instrumentation Supervisor (E and I Supervisor)

data.title: Computer, Automated Teller, and Office Machine Repairers
data.description: Repair, maintain, or install computers, word processing systems, automated teller machines, and electronic office machines, such as duplicating and fax machines

data.title: Radio, Cellular, and Tower Equipment Installers and Repairers
data.description: Repair, install, or maintain mobile or stationary radio transmitting, broadcasting, and receiving equipment, and two-way radio communications systems used in cellular telecommunications, mobile broadband, ship-to-shore, aircraft-to-ground communications, and radio equipment in service and emergency vehicles. May test and analyze network coverage

data.title: Telecommunications Equipment Installers and Repairers, Except Line Installers
data.description: Install, set up, rearrange, or remove switching, distribution, routing, and dialing equipment used in central offices or headends. Service or repair telephone, cable television, Internet, and other communications equipment on customers' property. May install communications equipment or communications wiring in buildings

data.title: Avionics Technicians
data.description: Install, inspect, test, adjust, or repair avionics equipment, such as radar, radio, navigation, and missile control systems in aircraft or space vehicles

data.title: Electric Motor, Power Tool, and Related Repairers
data.description: Repair, maintain, or install electric motors, wiring, or switches

data.title: Electrical and Electronics Installers and Repairers, Transportation Equipment
data.description: Install, adjust, or maintain mobile electronics communication equipment, including sound, sonar, security, navigation, and surveillance systems on trains, watercraft, or other mobile equipment

data.title: Electrical and Electronics Repairers, Commercial and Industrial Equipment
data.description: Repair, test, adjust, or install electronic equipment, such as industrial controls, transmitters, and antennas
alternate_titles: E and I Mechanic (Electrical and Instrument Mechanic)
E and I Mechanic (Electrical and Instrumentation Mechanic)
Electrical and Instrument Technician (E and I Tech)
I and C Tech (Instrument and Control Technician)
Instrument and Electrical Technician (I and E Tech)

data.title: Electrical and Electronics Repairers, Powerhouse, Substation, and Relay
data.description: Inspect, test, repair, or maintain electrical equipment in generating stations, substations, and in-service relays
alternate_titles: Instrument and Control Technician (I and C Technician)
Instrumentation and Control Technician (I and C Technician)

data.title: Electronic Equipment Installers and Repairers, Motor Vehicles
data.description: Install, diagnose, or repair communications, sound, security, or navigation equipment in motor vehicles

data.title: Audiovisual Equipment Installers and Repairers
data.description: Install, repair, or adjust audio or television receivers, stereo systems, camcorders, video systems, or other electronic entertainment equipment in homes or other venues. May perform routine maintenance
alternate_titles: A/V Installation Tech (Audio Visual Installation Technician)

data.title: Security and Fire Alarm Systems Installers
data.description: Install, program, maintain, and repair security and fire alarm wiring and equipment. Ensure that work is in accordance with relevant codes

data.title: Aircraft Mechanics and Service Technicians
data.description: Diagnose, adjust, repair, or overhaul aircraft engines and assemblies, such as hydraulic and pneumatic systems
alternate_titles: Airframe and Powerplant Mechanic (A and P Mechanic)

data.title: Automotive Body and Related Repairers
data.description: Repair and refinish automotive vehicle bodies and straighten vehicle frames
alternate_titles: Auto Body Repair Technician (Auto Body Repair Tech)

data.title: Automotive Glass Installers and Repairers
data.description: Replace or repair broken windshields and window glass in motor vehicles

data.title: Automotive Service Technicians and Mechanics
data.description: Diagnose, adjust, repair, or overhaul automotive vehicles

data.title: Bus and Truck Mechanics and Diesel Engine Specialists
data.description: Diagnose, adjust, repair, or overhaul buses and trucks, or maintain and repair any type of diesel engines. Includes mechanics working primarily with automobile or marine diesel engines

data.title: Farm Equipment Mechanics and Service Technicians
data.description: Diagnose, adjust, repair, or overhaul farm machinery and vehicles, such as tractors, harvesters, dairy equipment, and irrigation systems
alternate_titles: Farm Equipment Service Technician (Farm Equipment Service Tech)

data.title: Mobile Heavy Equipment Mechanics, Except Engines
data.description: Diagnose, adjust, repair, or overhaul mobile mechanical, hydraulic, and pneumatic equipment, such as cranes, bulldozers, graders, and conveyors, used in construction, logging, and mining

data.title: Rail Car Repairers
data.description: Diagnose, adjust, repair, or overhaul railroad rolling stock, mine cars, or mass transit rail cars

data.title: Motorboat Mechanics and Service Technicians
data.description: Repair and adjust electrical and mechanical equipment of inboard or inboard-outboard boat engines

data.title: Motorcycle Mechanics
data.description: Diagnose, adjust, repair, or overhaul motorcycles, scooters, mopeds, dirt bikes, or similar motorized vehicles

data.title: Outdoor Power Equipment and Other Small Engine Mechanics
data.description: Diagnose, adjust, repair, or overhaul small engines used to power lawn mowers, chain saws, recreational sporting equipment, and related equipment

data.title: Bicycle Repairers
data.description: Repair and service bicycles

data.title: Recreational Vehicle Service Technicians
data.description: Diagnose, inspect, adjust, repair, or overhaul recreational vehicles including travel trailers. Includes workers who perform customized van conversions
alternate_titles: Certified RV Technician (Certified Recreational Vehicle Technician)
Mobile Service RV Technician (Mobile Service Recreational Vehicle Technician)
RV Body Mechanic (Recreational Vehicle Body Mechanic)
RV Service Technician (Recreational Vehicle Service Technician)
RVDA Master Certified RV Technician (Recreational Vehicle Dealer Association Master Certified Recreational Vehicle Technician)

data.title: Tire Repairers and Changers
data.description: Repair and replace tires

data.title: Mechanical Door Repairers
data.description: Install, service, or repair automatic door mechanisms and hydraulic doors. Includes garage door mechanics

data.title: Control and Valve Installers and Repairers, Except Mechanical Door
data.description: Install, repair, and maintain mechanical regulating and controlling devices, such as electric meters, gas regulators, thermostats, safety and flow valves, and other mechanical governors
alternate_titles: Instrument and Electrical Technician (I and E Technician)

data.title: Heating, Air Conditioning, and Refrigeration Mechanics and Installers
data.description: Install or repair heating, central air conditioning, HVAC, or refrigeration systems, including oil burners, hot-air furnaces, and heating stoves
alternate_titles: HVAC Installer (Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning Installer)
HVAC Mechanic (Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning Mechanic)
HVAC Service Tech (Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning Service Technician)
HVAC Specialist (Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning Specialist)
HVAC Tech (Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning Technician)
