data.description: Repair, adjust, or install all types of electric or gas household appliances, such as refrigerators, washers, dryers, and ovens

data.title: Industrial Machinery Mechanics
data.description: Repair, install, adjust, or maintain industrial production and processing machinery or refinery and pipeline distribution systems. May also install, dismantle, or move machinery and heavy equipment according to plans

data.title: Maintenance Workers, Machinery
data.description: Lubricate machinery, change parts, or perform other routine machinery maintenance

data.title: Millwrights
data.description: Install, dismantle, or move machinery and heavy equipment according to layout plans, blueprints, or other drawings

data.title: Refractory Materials Repairers, Except Brickmasons
data.description: Build or repair equipment such as furnaces, kilns, cupolas, boilers, converters, ladles, soaking pits, and ovens, using refractory materials

data.title: Electrical Power-Line Installers and Repairers
data.description: Install or repair cables or wires used in electrical power or distribution systems. May erect poles and light or heavy duty transmission towers

data.title: Telecommunications Line Installers and Repairers
data.description: Install and repair telecommunications cable, including fiber optics
alternate_titles: Installation and Repair Technician (I and R Technician)

data.title: Camera and Photographic Equipment Repairers
data.description: Repair and adjust cameras and photographic equipment, including commercial video and motion picture camera equipment

data.title: Medical Equipment Repairers
data.description: Test, adjust, or repair biomedical or electromedical equipment
alternate_titles: Medical Equipment Service Tech (Medical Equipment Service Technician)

data.title: Musical Instrument Repairers and Tuners
data.description: Repair percussion, stringed, reed, or wind instruments. May specialize in one area, such as piano tuning
alternate_titles: Brass Instrument Repair Technician (Brass Instrument Repair Tech)
Musical Instrument Repair Technician (Musical Instrument Repair Tech)

data.title: Watch and Clock Repairers
data.description: Repair, clean, and adjust mechanisms of timing instruments, such as watches and clocks. Includes watchmakers, watch technicians, and mechanical timepiece repairers

data.title: Precision Instrument and Equipment Repairers, All Other
data.description: All precision instrument and equipment repairers not listed separately

data.title: Maintenance and Repair Workers, General
data.description: Perform work involving the skills of two or more maintenance or craft occupations to keep machines, mechanical equipment, or the structure of a building in repair. Duties may involve pipe fitting; HVAC maintenance; insulating; welding; machining; carpentry; repairing electrical or mechanical equipment; installing, aligning, and balancing new equipment; and repairing buildings, floors, or stairs

data.title: Wind Turbine Service Technicians
data.description: Inspect, diagnose, adjust, or repair wind turbines. Perform maintenance on wind turbine equipment including resolving electrical, mechanical, and hydraulic malfunctions

data.title: Coin, Vending, and Amusement Machine Servicers and Repairers
data.description: Install, service, adjust, or repair coin, vending, or amusement machines including video games, juke boxes, pinball machines, or slot machines

data.title: Commercial Divers
data.description: Work below surface of water, using surface-supplied air or scuba equipment to inspect, repair, remove, or install equipment and structures. May use a variety of power and hand tools, such as drills, sledgehammers, torches, and welding equipment. May conduct tests or experiments, rig explosives, or photograph structures or marine life
alternate_titles: Non Destructive Testing Under Water Welder (NDT U/W Welder)

data.title: Locksmiths and Safe Repairers
data.description: Repair and open locks, make keys, change locks and safe combinations, and install and repair safes

data.title: Manufactured Building and Mobile Home Installers
data.description: Move or install mobile homes or prefabricated buildings

data.title: Riggers
data.description: Set up or repair rigging for construction projects, manufacturing plants, logging yards, ships and shipyards, or for the entertainment industry

data.title: Signal and Track Switch Repairers
data.description: Install, inspect, test, maintain, or repair electric gate crossings, signals, signal equipment, track switches, section lines, or intercommunications systems within a railroad system

data.title: Helpers--Installation, Maintenance, and Repair Workers
data.description: Help installation, maintenance, and repair workers in maintenance, parts replacement, and repair of vehicles, industrial machinery, and electrical and electronic equipment. Perform duties such as furnishing tools, materials, and supplies to other workers; cleaning work area, machines, and tools; and holding materials or tools for other workers
alternate_titles: HVAC Helper (Heating, Ventilation, Air Conditioning Helper)
HVAC Installation Helper (Heating, Ventilation, Air Conditioning Installation Helper)

data.title: Installation, Maintenance, and Repair Workers, All Other
data.description: All installation, maintenance, and repair workers not listed separately

data.title: Geothermal Technicians
data.description: Perform technical activities at power plants or individual installations necessary for the generation of power from geothermal energy sources. Monitor and control operating activities at geothermal power generation facilities and perform maintenance and repairs as necessary. Install, test, and maintain residential and commercial geothermal heat pumps
alternate_titles: I and C Technician (Instrument and Controls Technician)
I and E Technician (Instrumentation and Electrical Technician)
I C and E Technician (Instrumentation, Control, and Electrical Technician)
Operations and Maintenance Technician (O and M Technician)

data.title: First-Line Supervisors of Production and Operating Workers
data.description: Directly supervise and coordinate the activities of production and operating workers, such as inspectors, precision workers, machine setters and operators, assemblers, fabricators, and plant and system operators. Excludes team or work leaders

data.title: Aircraft Structure, Surfaces, Rigging, and Systems Assemblers
data.description: Assemble, fit, fasten, and install parts of airplanes, space vehicles, or missiles, such as tails, wings, fuselage, bulkheads, stabilizers, landing gear, rigging and control equipment, or heating and ventilating systems

data.title: Coil Winders, Tapers, and Finishers
data.description: Wind wire coils used in electrical components, such as resistors and transformers, and in electrical equipment and instruments, such as field cores, bobbins, armature cores, electrical motors, generators, and control equipment

data.title: Electrical and Electronic Equipment Assemblers
data.description: Assemble or modify electrical or electronic equipment, such as computers, test equipment telemetering systems, electric motors, and batteries

data.title: Electromechanical Equipment Assemblers
data.description: Assemble or modify electromechanical equipment or devices, such as servomechanisms, gyros, dynamometers, magnetic drums, tape drives, brakes, control linkage, actuators, and appliances

data.title: Engine and Other Machine Assemblers
data.description: Construct, assemble, or rebuild machines, such as engines, turbines, and similar equipment used in such industries as construction, extraction, textiles, and paper manufacturing

data.title: Structural Metal Fabricators and Fitters
data.description: Fabricate, position, align, and fit parts of structural metal products

data.title: Fiberglass Laminators and Fabricators
data.description: Laminate layers of fiberglass on molds to form boat decks and hulls, bodies for golf carts, automobiles, or other products

data.title: Timing Device Assemblers and Adjusters
data.description: Perform precision assembling or adjusting, within narrow tolerances, of timing devices such as digital clocks or timing devices with electrical or electronic components

data.title: Team Assemblers
data.description: Work as part of a team having responsibility for assembling an entire product or component of a product. Team assemblers can perform all tasks conducted by the team in the assembly process and rotate through all or most of them, rather than being assigned to a specific task on a permanent basis. May participate in making management decisions affecting the work. Includes team leaders who work as pa

data.title: Assemblers and Fabricators, All Other
data.description: All assemblers and fabricators not listed separately

data.title: Bakers
data.description: Mix and bake ingredients to produce breads, rolls, cookies, cakes, pies, pastries, or other baked goods

data.title: Butchers and Meat Cutters
data.description: Cut, trim, or prepare consumer-sized portions of meat for use or sale in retail establishments

data.title: Meat, Poultry, and Fish Cutters and Trimmers
data.description: Use hands or hand tools to perform routine cutting and trimming of meat, poultry, and seafood

data.title: Slaughterers and Meat Packers
data.description: Perform nonroutine or precision functions involving the preparation of large portions of meat. Work may include specialized slaughtering tasks, cutting standard or premium cuts of meat for marketing, making sausage, or wrapping meats. Work typically occurs in slaughtering, meat packing, or wholesale establishments

data.title: Food and Tobacco Roasting, Baking, and Drying Machine Operators and Tenders
data.description: Operate or tend food or tobacco roasting, baking, or drying equipment, including hearth ovens, kiln driers, roasters, char kilns, and vacuum drying equipment

data.title: Food Batchmakers
data.description: Set up and operate equipment that mixes or blends ingredients used in the manufacturing of food products. Includes candy makers and cheese makers

data.title: Food Cooking Machine Operators and Tenders
data.description: Operate or tend cooking equipment, such as steam cooking vats, deep fry cookers, pressure cookers, kettles, and boilers, to prepare food products

data.title: Food Processing Workers, All Other
data.description: All food processing workers not listed separately

data.title: Extruding and Drawing Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders, Metal and Plastic
data.description: Set up, operate, or tend machines to extrude or draw thermoplastic or metal materials into tubes, rods, hoses, wire, bars, or structural shapes

data.title: Forging Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders, Metal and Plastic
data.description: Set up, operate, or tend forging machines to taper, shape, or form metal or plastic parts

data.title: Rolling Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders, Metal and Plastic
data.description: Set up, operate, or tend machines to roll steel or plastic forming bends, beads, knurls, rolls, or plate, or to flatten, temper, or reduce gauge of material

data.title: Cutting, Punching, and Press Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders, Metal and Plastic
data.description: Set up, operate, or tend machines to saw, cut, shear, slit, punch, crimp, notch, bend, or straighten metal or plastic material

data.title: Drilling and Boring Machine Tool Setters, Operators, and Tenders, Metal and Plastic
data.description: Set up, operate, or tend drilling machines to drill, bore, ream, mill, or countersink metal or plastic work pieces
alternate_titles: CNC Drilling Operator (Computer Numerical Control Drilling Operator)

data.title: Grinding, Lapping, Polishing, and Buffing Machine Tool Setters, Operators, and Tenders, Metal and Plastic
data.description: Set up, operate, or tend grinding and related tools that remove excess material or burrs from surfaces, sharpen edges or corners, or buff, hone, or polish metal or plastic work pieces

data.title: Lathe and Turning Machine Tool Setters, Operators, and Tenders, Metal and Plastic
data.description: Set up, operate, or tend lathe and turning machines to turn, bore, thread, form, or face metal or plastic materials, such as wire, rod, or bar stock
alternate_titles: CNC Lathe Operator (Computer Numerical Control Lathe Operator)
CNC Setup Operator (Computer Numerical Control Setup Operator)

data.title: Milling and Planing Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders, Metal and Plastic
data.description: Set up, operate, or tend milling or planing machines to mill, plane, shape, groove, or profile metal or plastic work pieces
alternate_titles: CNC Licensed Mill Operator (Computer Numerical Control Licensed Mill Operator)
CNC Mill Operator (Computer Numerical Control Mill Operator)
CNC Mill Operator (Computerized Numerical Control Mill Operator)
CNC Mill Set Up Operator (Computerized Numerical Control Mill Set Up Operator)

data.title: Machinists
data.description: Set up and operate a variety of machine tools to produce precision parts and instruments out of metal. Includes precision instrument makers who fabricate, modify, or repair mechanical instruments. May also fabricate and modify parts to make or repair machine tools or maintain industrial machines, applying knowledge of mechanics, mathematics, metal properties, layout, and machining procedures

data.title: Metal-Refining Furnace Operators and Tenders
data.description: Operate or tend furnaces, such as gas, oil, coal, electric-arc or electric induction, open-hearth, or oxygen furnaces, to melt and refine metal before casting or to produce specified types of steel
alternate_titles: Arc and Argon Oxygen Decarburization Melter (ARC and AOD Melter)

data.title: Pourers and Casters, Metal
data.description: Operate hand-controlled mechanisms to pour and regulate the flow of molten metal into molds to produce castings or ingots

data.title: Model Makers, Metal and Plastic
data.description: Set up and operate machines, such as lathes, milling and engraving machines, and jig borers to make working models of metal or plastic objects. Includes template makers

data.title: Patternmakers, Metal and Plastic
data.description: Lay out, machine, fit, and assemble castings and parts to metal or plastic foundry patterns, core boxes, or match plates

data.title: Foundry Mold and Coremakers
data.description: Make or form wax or sand cores or molds used in the production of metal castings in foundries

data.title: Molding, Coremaking, and Casting Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders, Metal and Plastic
data.description: Set up, operate, or tend metal or plastic molding, casting, or coremaking machines to mold or cast metal or thermoplastic parts or products

data.title: Multiple Machine Tool Setters, Operators, and Tenders, Metal and Plastic
data.description: Set up, operate, or tend more than one type of cutting or forming machine tool or robot
alternate_titles: CNC Machine Setter (Computer Numerically Controlled Machine Setter)

data.title: Tool and Die Makers
data.description: Analyze specifications, lay out metal stock, set up and operate machine tools, and fit and assemble parts to make and repair dies, cutting tools, jigs, fixtures, gauges, and machinists' hand tools

data.title: Welders, Cutters, Solderers, and Brazers
data.description: Use hand-welding, flame-cutting, hand-soldering, or brazing equipment to weld or join metal components or to fill holes, indentations, or seams of fabricated metal products

data.title: Welding, Soldering, and Brazing Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders
data.description: Set up, operate, or tend welding, soldering, or brazing machines or robots that weld, braze, solder, or heat treat metal products, components, or assemblies. Includes workers who operate laser cutters or laser-beam machines

data.title: Heat Treating Equipment Setters, Operators, and Tenders, Metal and Plastic
data.description: Set up, operate, or tend heating equipment, such as heat-treating furnaces, flame-hardening machines, induction machines, soaking pits, or vacuum equipment to temper, harden, anneal, or heat treat metal or plastic objects

data.title: Layout Workers, Metal and Plastic
data.description: Lay out reference points and dimensions on metal or plastic stock or workpieces, such as sheets, plates, tubes, structural shapes, castings, or machine parts, for further processing. Includes shipfitters

data.title: Plating Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders, Metal and Plastic
data.description: Set up, operate, or tend plating machines to coat metal or plastic products with chromium, zinc, copper, cadmium, nickel, or other metal to protect or decorate surfaces. Typically, the product being coated is immersed in molten metal or an electrolytic solution

data.title: Tool Grinders, Filers, and Sharpeners
data.description: Perform precision smoothing, sharpening, polishing, or grinding of metal objects
alternate_titles: OD Grinder Operator (Outer Diameter Grinder Operator)

data.title: Metal Workers and Plastic Workers, All Other
data.description: All metal workers and plastic workers not listed separately

data.title: Prepress Technicians and Workers
data.description: Format and proof text and images submitted by designers and clients into finished pages that can be printed. Includes digital and photo typesetting. May produce printing plates

data.title: Printing Press Operators
data.description: Set up and operate digital, letterpress, lithographic, flexographic, gravure, or other printing machines. Includes short-run offset printing presses

data.title: Print Binding and Finishing Workers
data.description: Bind books and other publications or finish printed products by hand or machine. May set up binding and finishing machines

data.title: Laundry and Dry-Cleaning Workers
data.description: Operate or tend washing or dry-cleaning machines to wash or dry-clean industrial or household articles, such as cloth garments, suede, leather, furs, blankets, draperies, linens, rugs, and carpets. Includes spotters and dyers of these articles

data.title: Pressers, Textile, Garment, and Related Materials
data.description: Press or shape articles by hand or machine

data.title: Sewing Machine Operators
data.description: Operate or tend sewing machines to join, reinforce, decorate, or perform related sewing operations in the manufacture of garment or nongarment products

data.title: Shoe and Leather Workers and Repairers
data.description: Construct, decorate, or repair leather and leather-like products, such as luggage, shoes, and saddles. May use hand tools

data.title: Shoe Machine Operators and Tenders
data.description: Operate or tend a variety of machines to join, decorate, reinforce, or finish shoes and shoe parts

data.title: Sewers, Hand
data.description: Sew, join, reinforce, or finish, usually with needle and thread, a variety of manufactured items. Includes weavers and stitchers

data.title: Tailors, Dressmakers, and Custom Sewers
data.description: Design, make, alter, repair, or fit garments

data.title: Textile Bleaching and Dyeing Machine Operators and Tenders
data.description: Operate or tend machines to bleach, shrink, wash, dye, or finish textiles or synthetic or glass fibers

data.title: Textile Cutting Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders
data.description: Set up, operate, or tend machines that cut textiles
alternate_titles: CNC Cutting Operator (Computer Numerical Control Cutting Operator)

data.title: Textile Knitting and Weaving Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders
data.description: Set up, operate, or tend machines that knit, loop, weave, or draw in textiles

data.title: Textile Winding, Twisting, and Drawing Out Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders
data.description: Set up, operate, or tend machines that wind or twist textiles; or draw out and combine sliver, such as wool, hemp, or synthetic fibers. Includes slubber machine and drawing frame operators

data.title: Extruding and Forming Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders, Synthetic and Glass Fibers
data.description: Set up, operate, or tend machines that extrude and form continuous filaments from synthetic materials, such as liquid polymer, rayon, and fiberglass

data.title: Fabric and Apparel Patternmakers
data.description: Draw and construct sets of precision master fabric patterns or layouts. May also mark and cut fabrics and apparel

data.title: Upholsterers
data.description: Make, repair, or replace upholstery for household furniture or transportation vehicles

data.title: Textile, Apparel, and Furnishings Workers, All Other
data.description: All textile, apparel, and furnishings workers not listed separately

data.title: Cabinetmakers and Bench Carpenters
data.description: Cut, shape, and assemble wooden articles or set up and operate a variety of woodworking machines, such as power saws, jointers, and mortisers to surface, cut, or shape lumber or to fabricate parts for wood products

data.title: Furniture Finishers
data.description: Shape, finish, and refinish damaged, worn, or used furniture or new high-grade furniture to specified color or finish

data.title: Model Makers, Wood
data.description: Construct full-size and scale wooden precision models of products. Includes wood jig builders and loft workers

data.title: Patternmakers, Wood
data.description: Plan, lay out, and construct wooden unit or sectional patterns used in forming sand molds for castings

data.title: Sawing Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders, Wood
data.description: Set up, operate, or tend wood sawing machines. May operate computer numerically controlled (CNC) equipment. Includes lead sawyers

data.title: Woodworking Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders, Except Sawing
data.description: Set up, operate, or tend woodworking machines, such as drill presses, lathes, shapers, routers, sanders, planers, and wood nailing machines. May operate computer numerically controlled (CNC) equipment

data.title: Woodworkers, All Other
data.description: All woodworkers not listed separately

data.title: Nuclear Power Reactor Operators
data.description: Operate or control nuclear reactors. Move control rods, start and stop equipment, monitor and adjust controls, and record data in logs. Implement emergency procedures when needed. May respond to abnormalities, determine cause, and recommend corrective action

data.title: Power Distributors and Dispatchers
data.description: Coordinate, regulate, or distribute electricity or steam

data.title: Power Plant Operators
data.description: Control, operate, or maintain machinery to generate electric power. Includes auxiliary equipment operators
alternate_titles: Operations and Maintenance Technician (O and M Tech)

data.title: Biomass Plant Technicians
data.description: Control and monitor biomass plant activities and perform maintenance as needed

data.title: Hydroelectric Plant Technicians
data.description: Monitor and control activities associated with hydropower generation. Operate plant equipment, such as turbines, pumps, valves, gates, fans, electric control boards, and battery banks. Monitor equipment operation and performance and make necessary adjustments to ensure optimal performance. Perform equipment maintenance and repair as necessary
alternate_titles: Hydroelectric Operations and Maintenance Technician (Hydro O and M Technician)

data.title: Stationary Engineers and Boiler Operators
data.description: Operate or maintain stationary engines, boilers, or other mechanical equipment to provide utilities for buildings or industrial processes. Operate equipment such as steam engines, generators, motors, turbines, and steam boilers

data.title: Water and Wastewater Treatment Plant and System Operators
data.description: Operate or control an entire process or system of machines, often through the use of control boards, to transfer or treat water or wastewater
alternate_titles: Waste Water Treatment Plant Operator (WWTP Operator)

data.title: Chemical Plant and System Operators
data.description: Control or operate entire chemical processes or system of machines

data.title: Gas Plant Operators
data.description: Distribute or process gas for utility companies and others by controlling compressors to maintain specified pressures on main pipelines
alternate_titles: Liquid Natural Gas Plant Operator (LNG Plant Operator)

data.title: Petroleum Pump System Operators, Refinery Operators, and Gaugers
data.description: Operate or control petroleum refining or processing units. May specialize in controlling manifold and pumping systems, gauging or testing oil in storage tanks, or regulating the flow of oil into pipelines

data.title: Plant and System Operators, All Other
data.description: All plant and system operators not listed separately

data.title: Biofuels Processing Technicians
data.description: Calculate, measure, load, mix, and process refined feedstock with additives in fermentation or reaction process vessels and monitor production process. Perform, and keep records of, plant maintenance, repairs, and safety inspections

data.title: Chemical Equipment Operators and Tenders
data.description: Operate or tend equipment to control chemical changes or reactions in the processing of industrial or consumer products. Equipment used includes devulcanizers, steam-jacketed kettles, and reactor vessels

data.title: Separating, Filtering, Clarifying, Precipitating, and Still Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders
data.description: Set up, operate, or tend continuous flow or vat-type equipment; filter presses; shaker screens; centrifuges; condenser tubes; precipitating, fermenting, or evaporating tanks; scrubbing towers; or batch stills. These machines extract, sort, or separate liquids, gases, or solids from other materials to recover a refined product. Includes dairy processing equipment operators

data.title: Crushing, Grinding, and Polishing Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders
data.description: Set up, operate, or tend machines to crush, grind, or polish materials, such as coal, glass, grain, stone, food, or rubber

data.title: Grinding and Polishing Workers, Hand
data.description: Grind, sand, or polish, using hand tools or hand-held power tools, a variety of metal, wood, stone, clay, plastic, or glass objects. Includes chippers, buffers, and finishers

data.title: Mixing and Blending Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders
data.description: Set up, operate, or tend machines to mix or blend materials, such as chemicals, tobacco, liquids, color pigments, or explosive ingredients

data.title: Cutters and Trimmers, Hand
data.description: Use hand tools or hand-held power tools to cut and trim a variety of manufactured items, such as carpet, fabric, stone, glass, or rubber

data.title: Cutting and Slicing Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders
data.description: Set up, operate, or tend machines that cut or slice materials, such as glass, stone, cork, rubber, tobacco, food, paper, or insulating material

data.title: Extruding, Forming, Pressing, and Compacting Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders
data.description: Set up, operate, or tend machines, such as glass-forming machines, plodder machines, and tuber machines, to shape and form products such as glassware, food, rubber, soap, brick, tile, clay, wax, tobacco, or cosmetics

data.title: Furnace, Kiln, Oven, Drier, and Kettle Operators and Tenders
data.description: Operate or tend heating equipment other than basic metal, plastic, or food processing equipment. Includes activities such as annealing glass, drying lumber, curing rubber, removing moisture from materials, or boiling soap

data.title: Inspectors, Testers, Sorters, Samplers, and Weighers
data.description: Inspect, test, sort, sample, or weigh nonagricultural raw materials or processed, machined, fabricated, or assembled parts or products for defects, wear, and deviations from specifications. May use precision measuring instruments and complex test equipment

data.title: Jewelers and Precious Stone and Metal Workers
data.description: Design, fabricate, adjust, repair, or appraise jewelry, gold, silver, other precious metals, or gems

data.title: Gem and Diamond Workers
data.description: Fabricate, finish, or evaluate the quality of gems and diamonds used in jewelry or industrial tools

data.title: Dental Laboratory Technicians
data.description: Construct and repair full or partial dentures or dental appliances
alternate_titles: Crown and Bridge Dental Laboratory Technician (Crown and Bridge Dental Lab Tech)

data.title: Medical Appliance Technicians
data.description: Construct, maintain, or repair medical supportive devices such as braces, orthotics and prosthetic devices, joints, arch supports, and other surgical and medical appliances
alternate_titles: Orthotic and Prosthetic Technician (O and P Technician)

data.title: Ophthalmic Laboratory Technicians
data.description: Cut, grind, and polish eyeglasses, contact lenses, or other precision optical elements. Assemble and mount lenses into frames or process other optical elements. Includes precision lens polishers or grinders, centerer-edgers, and lens mounters

data.title: Packaging and Filling Machine Operators and Tenders
data.description: Operate or tend machines to prepare industrial or consumer products for storage or shipment. Includes cannery workers who pack food products
alternate_titles: Computer Numerical Control Machine Operator (CNC Machine Operator)

data.title: Painting, Coating, and Decorating Workers
data.description: Paint, coat, or decorate articles, such as furniture, glass, plateware, pottery, jewelry, toys, books, or leather

data.title: Coating, Painting, and Spraying Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders
data.description: Set up, operate, or tend spraying or rolling machines to coat or paint any of a wide variety of products, including glassware, cloth, ceramics, metal, plastic, paper, or wood, with lacquer, silver, copper, rubber, varnish, glaze, enamel, oil, or rust-proofing materials. Includes painters of transportation vehicles such as painters in auto body repair facilities

data.title: Semiconductor Processing Technicians
data.description: Perform any or all of the following functions in the manufacture of electronic semiconductors: load semiconductor material into furnace; saw formed ingots into segments; load individual segment into crystal growing chamber and monitor controls; locate crystal axis in ingot using x-ray equipment and saw ingots into wafers; and clean, polish, and load wafers into series of special purpose furnaces,
alternate_titles: Metalorganic Chemical Vapor Deposition Engineer (MOCVD Engineer)

data.title: Photographic Process Workers and Processing Machine Operators
data.description: Perform work involved in developing and processing photographic images from film or digital media. May perform precision tasks such as editing photographic negatives and prints

data.title: Computer Numerically Controlled Tool Operators
data.description: Operate computer-controlled tools, machines, or robots to machine or process parts, tools, or other work pieces made of metal, plastic, wood, stone, or other materials. May also set up and maintain equipment
alternate_titles: CNC Gear Operator (Computer Numerical Control Gear Operator)
CNC Machine Operator (Computer Numerical Control Machine Operator)
CNC Set Up Operator (Computer Numerical Control Set Up Operator)

data.title: Computer Numerically Controlled Tool Programmers
data.description: Develop programs to control machining or processing of materials by automatic machine tools, equipment, or systems. May also set up, operate, or maintain equipment
alternate_titles: CNC Tool Programmer (Computer Numerical Control Tool Programmer)

data.title: Adhesive Bonding Machine Operators and Tenders
data.description: Operate or tend bonding machines that use adhesives to join items for further processing or to form a completed product. Processes include joining veneer sheets into plywood; gluing paper; or joining rubber and rubberized fabric parts, plastic, simulated leather, or other materials

data.title: Cleaning, Washing, and Metal Pickling Equipment Operators and Tenders
data.description: Operate or tend machines to wash or clean products, such as barrels or kegs, glass items, tin plate, food, pulp, coal, plastic, or rubber, to remove impurities

data.title: Cooling and Freezing Equipment Operators and Tenders
data.description: Operate or tend equipment such as cooling and freezing units, refrigerators, batch freezers, and freezing tunnels, to cool or freeze products, food, blood plasma, and chemicals

data.title: Etchers and Engravers
data.description: Engrave or etch metal, wood, rubber, or other materials. Includes such workers as etcher-circuit processors, pantograph engravers, and silk screen etchers

data.title: Molders, Shapers, and Casters, Except Metal and Plastic
data.description: Mold, shape, form, cast, or carve products such as food products, figurines, tile, pipes, and candles consisting of clay, glass, plaster, concrete, stone, or combinations of materials

data.title: Stone Cutters and Carvers, Manufacturing
data.description: Cut or carve stone according to diagrams and patterns

data.title: Glass Blowers, Molders, Benders, and Finishers
data.description: Shape molten glass according to patterns

data.title: Potters, Manufacturing
data.description: Operate production machines such as pug mill, jigger machine, or potter's wheel to process clay in manufacture of ceramic, pottery and stoneware products

data.title: Paper Goods Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders
data.description: Set up, operate, or tend paper goods machines that perform a variety of functions, such as converting, sawing, corrugating, banding, wrapping, boxing, stitching, forming, or sealing paper or paperboard sheets into products

data.title: Tire Builders
data.description: Operate machines to build tires

data.title: Helpers--Production Workers
data.description: Help production workers by performing duties requiring less skill. Duties include supplying or holding materials or tools, and cleaning work area and equipment

data.title: Production Workers, All Other
data.description: All production workers not listed separately

data.title: Aircraft Cargo Handling Supervisors
data.description: Supervise and coordinate the activities of ground crew in the loading, unloading, securing, and staging of aircraft cargo or baggage. May determine the quantity and orientation of cargo and compute aircraft center of gravity. May accompany aircraft as member of flight crew and monitor and handle cargo in flight, and assist and brief passengers on safety and emergency procedures. Includes loadmaste

data.title: First-Line Supervisors of Helpers, Laborers, and Material Movers, Hand
data.description: Directly supervise and coordinate the activities of helpers, laborers, or material movers, hand

data.title: Recycling Coordinators
data.description: Supervise curbside and drop-off recycling programs for municipal governments or private firms

data.title: First-Line Supervisors of Material-Moving Machine and Vehicle Operators
data.description: Directly supervise and coordinate activities of material-moving machine and vehicle operators and helpers

data.title: First-Line Supervisors of Passenger Attendants
data.description: Supervise and coordinate activities of passenger attendants

data.title: First-Line Supervisors of Transportation Workers, All Other
data.description: All first-line supervisors of transportation workers not listed separately

data.title: Airline Pilots, Copilots, and Flight Engineers
data.description: Pilot and navigate the flight of fixed-wing aircraft, usually on scheduled air carrier routes, for the transport of passengers and cargo. Requires Federal Air Transport certificate and rating for specific aircraft type used. Includes regional, national, and international airline pilots and flight instructors of airline pilots

data.title: Commercial Pilots
data.description: Pilot and navigate the flight of fixed-wing aircraft on nonscheduled air carrier routes, or helicopters. Requires Commercial Pilot certificate. Includes charter pilots with similar certification, and air ambulance and air tour pilots. Excludes regional, national, and international airline pilots
alternate_titles: EMS Helicopter Pilot (Emergency Medical Service Helicopter Pilot)

data.title: Air Traffic Controllers
data.description: Control air traffic on and within vicinity of airport, and movement of air traffic between altitude sectors and control centers, according to established procedures and policies. Authorize, regulate, and control commercial airline flights according to government or company regulations to expedite and ensure flight safety
alternate_titles: Terminal Air Traffic Control Specialist (Terminal ATC Specialist)

data.title: Airfield Operations Specialists
data.description: Ensure the safe takeoff and landing of commercial and military aircraft. Duties include coordination between air-traffic control and maintenance personnel, dispatching, using airfield landing and navigational aids, implementing airfield safety procedures, monitoring and maintaining flight records, and applying knowledge of weather information

data.title: Flight Attendants
data.description: Monitor safety of the aircraft cabin. Provide services to airline passengers, explain safety information, serve food and beverages, and respond to emergency incidents

data.title: Ambulance Drivers and Attendants, Except Emergency Medical Technicians
data.description: Drive ambulance or assist ambulance driver in transporting sick, injured, or convalescent persons. Assist in lifting patients
alternate_titles: CPR Ambulance Driver (Cardio Pulmonary Resuscitation Ambulance Driver)

data.title: Driver/Sales Workers
data.description: Drive truck or other vehicle over established routes or within an established territory and sell or deliver goods, such as food products, including restaurant take-out items, or pick up or deliver items such as commercial laundry. May also take orders, collect payment, or stock merchandise at point of delivery

data.title: Heavy and Tractor-Trailer Truck Drivers
data.description: Drive a tractor-trailer combination or a truck with a capacity of at least 26,001 pounds Gross Vehicle Weight (GVW). May be required to unload truck. Requires commercial drivers' license. Includes tow truck drivers

data.title: Light Truck Drivers
data.description: Drive a light vehicle, such as a truck or van, with a capacity of less than 26,001 pounds Gross Vehicle Weight (GVW), primarily to pick up merchandise or packages from a distribution center and deliver. May load and unload vehicle

data.title: Bus Drivers, School
data.description: Drive a school bus to transport students. Ensure adherence to safety rules. May assist students in boarding or exiting
alternate_titles: CDL Bus Driver (Commercial Driver's License Bus Driver)
SPED Bus Driver (Special Education Bus Driver)
SPED School Bus Driver (Special Education School Bus Driver)

data.title: Bus Drivers, Transit and Intercity
data.description: Drive bus or motor coach, including regular route operations, charters, and private carriage. May assist passengers with baggage. May collect fares or tickets

data.title: Shuttle Drivers and Chauffeurs
data.description: Drive a motor vehicle to transport passengers on a planned or scheduled basis. May collect a fare. Includes nonemergency medical transporters and hearse drivers

data.title: Taxi Drivers
data.description: Drive a motor vehicle to transport passengers on an unplanned basis and charge a fare, usually based on a meter

data.title: Motor Vehicle Operators, All Other
data.description: All motor vehicle operators not listed separately

data.title: Locomotive Engineers
data.description: Drive electric, diesel-electric, steam, or gas-turbine-electric locomotives to transport passengers or freight. Interpret train orders, electronic or manual signals, and railroad rules and regulations

data.title: Rail Yard Engineers, Dinkey Operators, and Hostlers
data.description: Drive switching or other locomotive or dinkey engines within railroad yard, industrial plant, quarry, construction project, or similar location

data.title: Railroad Brake, Signal, and Switch Operators and Locomotive Firers
data.description: Operate or monitor railroad track switches or locomotive instruments. May couple or uncouple rolling stock to make up or break up trains. Watch for and relay traffic signals. May inspect couplings, air hoses, journal boxes, and hand brakes. May watch for dragging equipment or obstacles on rights-of-way

data.title: Railroad Conductors and Yardmasters
data.description: Coordinate activities of switch-engine crew within railroad yard, industrial plant, or similar location. Conductors coordinate activities of train crew on passenger or freight trains. Yardmasters review train schedules and switching orders and coordinate activities of workers engaged in railroad traffic operations, such as the makeup or breakup of trains and yard switching

data.title: Subway and Streetcar Operators
data.description: Operate subway or elevated suburban trains with no separate locomotive, or electric-powered streetcar, to transport passengers. May handle fares

data.title: Rail Transportation Workers, All Other
data.description: All rail transportation workers not listed separately

data.title: Sailors and Marine Oilers
data.description: Stand watch to look for obstructions in path of vessel, measure water depth, turn wheel on bridge, or use emergency equipment as directed by captain, mate, or pilot. Break out, rig, overhaul, and store cargo-handling gear, stationary rigging, and running gear. Perform a variety of maintenance tasks to preserve the painted surface of the ship and to maintain line and ship equipment. Must hold gover

data.title: Captains, Mates, and Pilots of Water Vessels
data.description: Command or supervise operations of ships and water vessels, such as tugboats and ferryboats. Required to hold license issued by U.S. Coast Guard

data.title: Motorboat Operators
data.description: Operate small motor-driven boats. May assist in navigational activities

data.title: Ship Engineers
data.description: Supervise and coordinate activities of crew engaged in operating and maintaining engines, boilers, deck machinery, and electrical, sanitary, and refrigeration equipment aboard ship

data.title: Bridge and Lock Tenders
data.description: Operate and tend bridges, canal locks, and lighthouses to permit marine passage on inland waterways, near shores, and at danger points in waterway passages. May supervise such operations. Includes drawbridge operators, lock operators, and slip bridge operators

data.title: Parking Attendants
data.description: Park vehicles or issue tickets for customers in a parking lot or garage. May park or tend vehicles in environments such as a car dealership or rental car facility. May collect fee

data.title: Automotive and Watercraft Service Attendants
data.description: Service automobiles, buses, trucks, boats, and other automotive or marine vehicles with fuel, lubricants, and accessories. Collect payment for services and supplies. May lubricate vehicle, change motor oil, refill antifreeze, or replace lights or other accessories, such as windshield wiper blades or fan belts. May repair or replace tires

data.title: Aircraft Service Attendants
data.description: Service aircraft with fuel. May de-ice aircraft, refill water and cooling agents, empty sewage tanks, service air and oxygen systems, or clean and polish exterior

data.title: Traffic Technicians
data.description: Conduct field studies to determine traffic volume, speed, effectiveness of signals, adequacy of lighting, and other factors influencing traffic conditions, under direction of traffic engineer

data.title: Transportation Inspectors
data.description: Inspect equipment or goods in connection with the safe transport of cargo or people. Includes rail transportation inspectors, such as freight inspectors, rail inspectors, and other inspectors of transportation vehicles not elsewhere classified

data.title: Aviation Inspectors
data.description: Inspect aircraft, maintenance procedures, air navigational aids, air traffic controls, and communications equipment to ensure conformance with Federal safety regulations
alternate_titles: Aircraft Quality Control Inspector (Aircraft QC Inspector)

data.title: Transportation Vehicle, Equipment and Systems Inspectors, Except Aviation
data.description: Inspect and monitor transportation equipment, vehicles, or systems to ensure compliance with regulations and safety standards

data.title: Passenger Attendants
data.description: Provide services to ensure the safety of passengers aboard ships, buses, trains, or within the station or terminal. Perform duties such as explaining the use of safety equipment, serving meals or beverages, or answering questions related to travel

data.title: Transportation Workers, All Other
data.description: All transportation workers not listed separately

data.title: Conveyor Operators and Tenders
data.description: Control or tend conveyors or conveyor systems that move materials or products to and from stockpiles, processing stations, departments, or vehicles. May control speed and routing of materials or products

data.title: Crane and Tower Operators
data.description: Operate mechanical boom and cable or tower and cable equipment to lift and move materials, machines, or products in many directions

data.title: Dredge Operators
data.description: Operate dredge to remove sand, gravel, or other materials in order to excavate and maintain navigable channels in waterways

data.title: Hoist and Winch Operators
data.description: Operate or tend hoists or winches to lift and pull loads using power-operated cable equipment

data.title: Industrial Truck and Tractor Operators
data.description: Operate industrial trucks or tractors equipped to move materials around a warehouse, storage yard, factory, construction site, or similar location

data.title: Cleaners of Vehicles and Equipment
data.description: Wash or otherwise clean vehicles, machinery, and other equipment. Use such materials as water, cleaning agents, brushes, cloths, and hoses

data.title: Laborers and Freight, Stock, and Material Movers, Hand
data.description: Manually move freight, stock, luggage, or other materials, or perform other general labor. Includes all manual laborers not elsewhere classified

data.title: Recycling and Reclamation Workers
data.description: Prepare and sort materials or products for recycling. Identify and remove hazardous substances. Dismantle components of products such as appliances
alternate_titles: Convenience Recycle Center Technician (Convenience Recycle Center Tech)
Deconstruction and Decontamination Waste Operations Specialist (D and D Waste Operations Specialist)

data.title: Machine Feeders and Offbearers
data.description: Feed materials into or remove materials from machines or equipment that is automatic or tended by other workers

data.title: Packers and Packagers, Hand
data.description: Pack or package by hand a wide variety of products and materials

data.title: Stockers and Order Fillers
data.description: Receive, store, and issue merchandise, materials, equipment, and other items from stockroom, warehouse, or storage yard to fill shelves, racks, tables, or customers' orders. May operate power equipment to fill orders. May mark prices on merchandise and set up sales displays

data.title: Gas Compressor and Gas Pumping Station Operators
data.description: Operate steam-, gas-, electric motor-, or internal combustion-engine driven compressors. Transmit, compress, or recover gases, such as butane, nitrogen, hydrogen, and natural gas
alternate_titles: Liquefied Natural Gas Plant Operator (LNG Plant Operator)

data.title: Pump Operators, Except Wellhead Pumpers
data.description: Tend, control, or operate power-driven, stationary, or portable pumps and manifold systems to transfer gases, oil, other liquids, slurries, or powdered materials to and from various vessels and processes

data.title: Wellhead Pumpers
data.description: Operate power pumps and auxiliary equipment to produce flow of oil or gas from wells in oil field

data.title: Refuse and Recyclable Material Collectors
data.description: Collect and dump refuse or recyclable materials from containers into truck. May drive truck

data.title: Tank Car, Truck, and Ship Loaders
data.description: Load and unload chemicals and bulk solids, such as coal, sand, and grain, into or from tank cars, trucks, or ships, using material moving equipment. May perform a variety of other tasks relating to shipment of products. May gauge or sample shipping tanks and test them for leaks

data.title: Material Moving Workers, All Other
data.description: All material moving workers not listed separately

data.title: Air Crew Officers
data.description: Perform and direct in-flight duties to ensure the successful completion of combat, reconnaissance, transport, and search and rescue missions. Duties include operating aircraft communications and radar equipment, such as establishing satellite linkages and jamming enemy communications capabilities; operating aircraft weapons and defensive systems; conducting preflight, in-flight, and postflight ins

data.title: Aircraft Launch and Recovery Officers
data.description: Plan and direct the operation and maintenance of catapults, arresting gear, and associated mechanical, hydraulic, and control systems involved primarily in aircraft carrier takeoff and landing operations. Duties include supervision of readiness and safety of arresting gear, launching equipment, barricades, and visual landing aid systems; planning and coordinating the design, development, and testi

data.title: Armored Assault Vehicle Officers
data.description: Direct the operation of tanks, light armor, and amphibious assault vehicle units during combat situations on land or in aquatic environments. Duties include directing crew members in the operation of targeting and firing systems; coordinating the operation of advanced onboard communications and navigation equipment; directing the transport of personnel and equipment during combat; formulating and

data.title: Artillery and Missile Officers
data.description: Manage personnel and weapons operations to destroy enemy positions, aircraft, and vessels. Duties include planning, targeting, and coordinating the tactical deployment of field artillery and air defense artillery missile systems units; directing the establishment and operation of fire control communications systems; targeting and launching intercontinental ballistic missiles; directing the storage

data.title: Command and Control Center Officers
data.description: Manage the operation of communications, detection, and weapons systems essential for controlling air, ground, and naval operations. Duties include managing critical communication links between air, naval, and ground forces; formulating and implementing emergency plans for natural and wartime disasters; coordinating emergency response teams and agencies; evaluating command center information and ne

data.title: Infantry Officers
data.description: Direct, train, and lead infantry units in ground combat operations. Duties include directing deployment of infantry weapons, vehicles, and equipment; directing location, construction, and camouflage of infantry positions and equipment; managing field communications operations; coordinating with armor, artillery, and air support units; performing strategic and tactical planning, including battle pl

data.title: Special Forces Officers
data.description: Lead elite teams that implement unconventional operations by air, land, or sea during combat or peacetime. These activities include offensive raids, demolitions, reconnaissance, search and rescue, and counterterrorism. In addition to their combat training, special forces officers often have specialized training in swimming, diving, parachuting, survival, emergency medicine, and foreign languages.

data.title: Military Officer Special and Tactical Operations Leaders, All Other
data.description: All military officer special and tactical operations leaders not listed separately

data.title: First-Line Supervisors of Air Crew Members
data.description: Supervise and coordinate the activities of air crew members. Supervisors may also perform the same activities as the workers they supervise

data.title: First-Line Supervisors of Weapons Specialists/Crew Members
data.description: Supervise and coordinate the activities of weapons specialists/crew members. Supervisors may also perform the same activities as the workers they supervise

data.title: First-Line Supervisors of All Other Tactical Operations Specialists
data.description: Supervise and coordinate the activities of all other tactical operations specialists not classified separately above. Supervisors may also perform the same activities as the workers they supervise

data.title: Air Crew Members
data.description: Perform in-flight duties to ensure the successful completion of combat, reconnaissance, transport, and search and rescue missions. Duties include operating aircraft communications and detection equipment, including establishing satellite linkages and jamming enemy communications capabilities; conducting preflight, in-flight, and postflight inspections of onboard equipment; operating and maintainin

data.title: Aircraft Launch and Recovery Specialists
data.description: Operate and maintain catapults, arresting gear, and associated mechanical, hydraulic, and control systems involved primarily in aircraft carrier takeoff and landing operations. Duties include installing and maintaining visual landing aids; testing and maintaining launch and recovery equipment using electric and mechanical test equipment and hand tools; activating airfield arresting systems, such a

Field artillery crew members predominantly use guns, cannons, and howitzers in ground combat operations, while air defense artillery crew members predominantly use missiles and rockets. data.title: Infantry
data.description: Operate weapons and equipment in ground combat operations. Duties include operating and maintaining weapons, such as rifles, machine guns, mortars, and hand grenades; locating, constructing, and camouflaging infantry positions and equipment; evaluating terrain and recording topographical information; operating and maintaining field communications equipment; assessing need for and directing support

data.title: Special Forces

These activities include offensive raids, demolitions, reconnaissance, search and rescue, and counterterrorism. In addition to their combat training, special forces members often have specialized training in swimming, diving, parachuting, survival, emergency medicine, and foreign language

data.title: Military Enlisted Tactical Operations and Air/Weapons Specialists and Crew Members, All Other
data.description: All military enlisted tactical operations and air/weapons specialists and crewmembers not listed separately
